event_description: Iranian cyberattacks target US financial systems and Gulf state infrastructure: US Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) reports coordinated cyberattacks attributed to APT33 (Elfin) targeting US financial sector networks, Gulf state water treatment systems, and Israeli power grid control systems. Most attacks are mitigated. CISA raises threat level to 'Shields Up' — the highest alert tier.
timeline_data_point: CISA 'Shields Up' alert issued

event_description: President Trump addresses the nation: Trump announces Operation Epic Fury from the Oval Office. Calls it 'a decisive action to prevent a nuclear Iran.' Pledges 'no ground troops' and 'total air dominance.'
Oil markets surge: Brent crude spikes 18% in early trading as markets absorb the reality of a major Middle East conflict. Trading halted briefly on circuit breakers. WTI follows with 16% jump.
timeline_data_point: Brent +18% in first trading session

event_description: Minab girls' school struck — 165+ children and staff killed: US Tomahawk cruise missile strikes Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school in Minab, adjacent to an IRGC naval complex, during the opening wave at approximately 10:45 AM local time. 165-180 killed, mostly girls aged 7-12, plus teachers and parents. The school was triple-tapped per Iranian officials. Munitions experts confirm the weapon as a US Tomahawk. Pentagon preliminary investigation finds outdated DIA intelligence failed to distinguish the school from the military compound.
timeline_source: Iranian Red Crescent / NYT / CNN / BBC Verify / Bellingcat / OHCHR
timeline_data_point: 165-180 killed — deadliest civilian casualty incident

event_description: Day 1 estimated killed — Iran (military): 65. Context: Day 1 — First strikes, Minab school (165+ killed), Iranian retaliation
Day 1 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 5. Context: Day 1 — First strikes, Minab school (165+ killed), Iranian retaliation
Day 1 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 1 — First strikes, Minab school (165+ killed), Iranian retaliation

Day 1 estimated killed — Israel (military): 0. Context: Day 1 — First strikes, Minab school (165+ killed), Iranian retaliation

6 US soldiers killed at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait: A projectile penetrates air defenses and directly hits a makeshift tactical operations center at Port Shuaiba, Kuwait. Six US Army Reserve soldiers from the 103rd Sustainment Command (Des Moines, Iowa) are killed. First US combat deaths of the conflict.
timeline_source: DoD / CENTCOM / CNN / Stars and Stripes
timeline_data_point: 6 KIA — deadliest single attack on US forces

event_description: USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) — ENGAGED — Primary strike platform — deep strike into Iran and attrition of Iranian naval capabilities
location_name: Arabian Sea — continuous combat ops — CENTCOM confirms 11,000+ US targets struck
strike_notes: Day 30: Sole operational carrier in direct theater. CENTCOM: 11,000+ US targets struck, 66% of missile/drone/naval production destroyed, 8,000+ combat flights, 850+ Tomahawks expended, 150+ Iranian vessels sunk. CSG-3 with CVW-9 and DESRON 21 + 6 independently deployed DDGs.
naval_aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, F-35C Lightning II (VMFA-314), EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, CMV-22B Osprey, MH-60R/S Seahawk
naval_escorts: DDG-121 USS Frank E. Petersen Jr.; DDG-111 USS Spruance; DDG-112 USS Michael Murphy; CG-70 USS Lake Erie

event_description: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) — PORT VISIT — SPLIT, CROATIA — Port visit and crew rest in Split, Croatia after 277-day deployment; fire damage repairs ongoing
location_name: Split, Croatia — port visit/crew rest after 277-day deployment; fire damage repairs
strike_notes: Day 30: Port visit in Split, Croatia (Mar 28) for crew rest after 277-day deployment. Fire damage repairs from March 12 non-combat laundry room fire (~200 treated for smoke inhalation). Bush CSG reportedly heading to Mediterranean to relieve Ford.
naval_aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye
naval_escorts: DDG-96 USS Bainbridge; DDG-72 USS Mahan; DDG-81 USS Winston S. Churchill

event_description: USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) — PRE-DEPLOYMENT — Pre-deployment workups — expected to relieve Ford; completing exercises
location_name: Naval Station Norfolk, VA — NOT yet deployed
strike_notes: Day 29: Completed COMPTUEX; certified combat-ready. Reported heading to Mediterranean to relieve Ford. No official DoD confirmation of CENTCOM orders.
naval_aircraft: F/A-18E/F Super Hornets, EA-18G Growlers, E-2D Hawkeye, MH-60R/S Seahawk

event_description: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron (LCS-30 / LCS-16 / LCS-32) — MCM OPERATIONS — BAHRAIN — Mine countermeasure operations in Strait of Hormuz from Bahrain
location_name: Bahrain / Strait of Hormuz — conducting mine countermeasures
military_asset: Bahrain LCS MCM Squadron (LCS-30 / LCS-16 / LCS-32)
strike_notes: Day 30: Three LCS conducting MCM from Bahrain per manifest. UK preparing autonomous minehunting capability aboard RFA Lyme Bay and HMS Brocklesby. French company Exail offered 40 unmanned MCM systems. Iran deployed 12+ mines (Maham-3 moored, Maham-7 bottom). CENTCOM destroyed 44+ Iranian minelayers.

event_description: USS Florida (SSGN-728) — DEPLOYED — Tomahawk cruise missile strike platform
location_name: Gulf of Oman / Arabian Sea (approx.) — exact position classified
strike_notes: Day 23: Ohio-class SSGN. Carries up to 154 Tomahawk cruise missiles. Submarine operations remain classified — position is approximate/notional. Weapons confirmed in use include Tomahawk Block Va Maritime Strike variant with radar-absorbent 'black' coating. CENTCOM confirms 8,000+ US targets struck cumulative.

event_description: USS John Finn (DDG-113) — ENGAGED — Air defense, escort operations, strike support
location_name: Arabian Sea — operating with CSG-3 destroyer screen
strike_notes: Day 23: Part of expanded ~12-14 destroyer presence in region. USS McFaul (DDG-74), USS Milius (DDG-69), USS Delbert D. Black (DDG-119), USS Pinckney (DDG-91), USS Mitscher (DDG-57), USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112), plus 4 DDGs in Eastern Med. Total: ~12-14 destroyers in region.

event_description: FS Charles de Gaulle (R91) — DEPLOYED — French carrier operations — Eastern Mediterranean. France pledged 8 frigates + 2 amphibious assault ships.
strike_notes: Day 30: French CSG operating in Eastern Mediterranean with Spanish and Dutch escorts. France pledged 8 frigates + 2 amphibious assault ships for coalition operations.

event_description: Operation Epic Fury begins: US and Israeli forces launch coordinated strikes on Iranian nuclear facilities, IRGC command centers, and air defense networks. Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei killed in strike on Leadership House compound in Tehran.
timeline_data_point: 3,000+ targets struck in first 72 hours

event_description: Iran retaliates on Gulf states and Israel: IRGC Aerospace Force launches ballistic missile salvos at US bases in Kuwait, Qatar, UAE, and Bahrain. Simultaneous strikes on Israeli military targets including Nevatim Air Base and Ben Gurion Airport perimeter.
timeline_data_point: 200+ missiles launched in first wave

event_description: Iraqi militias launch rocket attacks on US bases in Iraq and Syria: Kata'ib Hezbollah and allied PMF factions launch coordinated rocket and drone attacks on Al-Asad Air Base in western Iraq, Erbil in Kurdistan, and Al-Tanf garrison in Syria. US CRAM systems engage incoming. Three US service members wounded at Al-Asad.
timeline_data_point: 3 US bases struck simultaneously

event_description: Iran launches Shahed drone swarms at Gulf military bases: IRGC launches waves of Shahed-136 one-way attack drones targeting US and coalition bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Qatar. Most are intercepted by Patriot, NASAMS, and CRAM systems. The volume of launches — estimated 150+ in the first 24 hours — strains air defense interceptor stocks.
timeline_data_point: 150+ Shahed drones launched in first 24 hours

event_description: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
location_name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
casualties_reported: Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
strike_notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

event_description: Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar: Shahab-3 MRBM / cruise missiles — Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
casualties_reported: Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
strike_notes: CENTCOM forward HQ. Qatar Defence Ministry said it thwarted attacks per pre-approved security plan. Two missiles reached base on March 1 per Qatar's official news agency. Long-range early warning radar in northern Qatar also targeted. Dozens of explosions heard in Doha from Patriot intercepts.

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
casualties_reported: 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
casualties_reported: 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
casualties_reported: 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Nevatim Air Base, Israel: Ballistic missiles — Runway damage, aircraft losses not confirmed
casualties_reported: Runway damage, aircraft losses not confirmed
strike_notes: Home of Israeli F-35I Adir squadron. Priority target for Iran. [NOTE: Nevatim was confirmed targeted in 2024 and 2025 Iran-Israel exchanges. Targeting during 2026 conflict is plausible but specific damage reports for this war unverified in open-source reporting as of March 13.]

event_description: Dimona area, Israel: Shahab-3 MRBM — All intercepted — no impact
strike_notes: Symbolic targeting near Israeli nuclear facility. Arrow-3 exoatmospheric intercepts. [NOTE: Symbolic targeting plausible given precedent. Arrow-3 intercepts plausible. But specific confirmed reporting of Dimona-area strikes during 2026 war not found in open sources as of March 13.]

event_description: Camp Buehring, Kuwait: Drones — 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
casualties_reported: 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
strike_notes: Northeastern Kuwait. Iranian drone struck the US garrison. Maj. Davius died during medical emergency at Camp Buehring on March 6 — incident under investigation, classified as health-related, not combat. IRGC claimed 230-drone wave targeting Camp Buehring among other Kuwait facilities on Day 6.

event_description: Jordan (multiple locations): Ballistic missiles / drones — 14 people injured
strike_notes: Jordan intercepted 2 ballistic missiles on Feb 28. Total of 119 Iranian missiles and drones targeted Jordan. 14 people injured. Jordan has historically cooperated with US/Israeli air defense operations.

event_description: Kuwait International Airport: Drone / missile — Minor injuries to airport workers. Limited material damage to passenger terminal. 1 girl later died from shrapnel injuries (March 4). Radar equipment damaged March 16.
casualties_reported: Minor injuries to airport workers. Limited material damage to passenger terminal. 1 girl later died from shrapnel injuries (March 4). Radar equipment damaged March 16.
strike_notes: Civilian airport struck by drone on evening of Feb 28. Kuwaiti authorities confirmed minor injuries and damage to passenger building. On March 4, Kuwaiti Ministry of Health reported death of a girl from shrapnel-induced injuries — likely related to airport or broader Kuwait strikes. On March 16, radar equipment damaged by Iranian strike.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
casualties_reported: 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: B-2 Spirits lead opening strikes on hardened targets: B-2 Spirit stealth bombers flying from Whiteman AFB, Missouri deliver GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators on Fordow and other hardened nuclear sites. B-52Hs operating from Diego Garcia and Al Udeid launch cruise missile salvos at IRGC command centers. The air campaign involves assets from 3 carrier strike groups, multiple bomber wings, and F-35s operating from Gulf bases.
timeline_data_point: 3 carrier strike groups, B-2 and B-52 wings deployed

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Isfahan: Natanz uranium enrichment facility
strike_notes: Primary nuclear targets struck in first wave. Deep penetrating munitions used. Day 22 (Mar 21): Natanz Shahid Ahmadi-Roshan Enrichment Complex struck with bunker-buster bombs (likely GBU-57 MOPs and/or GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrators). Israel denied involvement ('did not conduct any strikes in the area'). IAEA: 'No increase in off-site radiation levels.' DG Grossi called for 'military restraint to avoid any risk of nuclear accident.' Russia condemned as 'blatant violation of international law.' Entrance buildings of underground Fuel Enrichment Plant damaged. Bulk of Iran's enriched uranium (~440 kg) believed at Isfahan, not Natanz. Third strike on Natanz (after June 2025 Operation Midnight Hammer and early March 2026).

strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. event_description: Minab: IRGC Navy 'Sayyid al-Shuhada' military complex / Asif missile brigade HQ (intended target)
strike_notes: Deadliest confirmed civilian casualty incident of the war. Struck at approximately 10:45 AM local time on Day 1 during opening wave. School was triple-tapped per Iranian officials (mayor of Minab, Ministry of Education). Victims were primarily girls aged 7–12, plus teachers, parents, and the school principal. School had been physically separated from adjacent IRGC naval base by a wall and separate entrance since ~2016, with a visible soccer pitch in the courtyard by 2017 (confirmed by satellite imagery reviewed by HRW, NYT, CNN, TIME). Bellingcat and BBC Verify geolocated footage of a US Tomahawk cruise missile striking the area. Eight munitions experts confirmed to the Washington Post the missile was a Tomahawk. Pentagon preliminary internal investigation found US was likely responsible due to outdated DIA intelligence that failed to distinguish the school from the military compound. CNN reported CENTCOM created target coordinates using outdated DIA information. Trump initially blamed Iran; munitions experts confirmed no Iranian missile matches the weapon in verified footage. US is the only belligerent operating Tomahawk missiles. Under formal Pentagon investigation expected to take months.

event_description: Minab: Shajareh Tayyebeh girls' elementary school (struck directly — adjacent to but walled off from IRGC base since 2016)
strike_notes: Deadliest confirmed civilian casualty incident of the war. Struck at approximately 10:45 AM local time on Day 1 during opening wave. School was triple-tapped per Iranian officials (mayor of Minab, Ministry of Education). Victims were primarily girls aged 7–12, plus teachers, parents, and the school principal. School had been physically separated from adjacent IRGC naval base by a wall and separate entrance since ~2016, with a visible soccer pitch in the courtyard by 2017 (confirmed by satellite imagery reviewed by HRW, NYT, CNN, TIME). Bellingcat and BBC Verify geolocated footage of a US Tomahawk cruise missile striking the area. Eight munitions experts confirmed to the Washington Post the missile was a Tomahawk. Pentagon preliminary internal investigation found US was likely responsible due to outdated DIA intelligence that failed to distinguish the school from the military compound. CNN reported CENTCOM created target coordinates using outdated DIA information. Trump initially blamed Iran; munitions experts confirmed no Iranian missile matches the weapon in verified footage. US is the only belligerent operating Tomahawk missiles. Under formal Pentagon investigation expected to take months.

event_description: Qom: Military and government sites in Qom city proper (distinct from Fordow enrichment facility)
strike_notes: Qom city listed as a Day 1 target alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Karaj, Kermanshah, and Kerman. Fordow enrichment facility (near Qom) tracked separately. Continued strikes reported in subsequent days.

event_description: UN Security Council emergency session: Emergency UNSC meeting called. Russia and China condemn US strikes. US and UK defend action under self-defense and non-proliferation mandates. No resolution passed.

Saudi Arabia condemns both Iran and US strikes — calls for restraint: Saudi Arabia issues carefully worded statement through the Foreign Ministry condemning 'all acts that threaten regional stability' without naming either party. Riyadh privately grants expanded US basing and overflight rights while publicly maintaining distance. Crown Prince MBS speaks with President Trump and Chinese President Xi on the same day.

Iraq declares neutrality — closes airspace to US combat operations: Iraqi Prime Minister announces Iraq will not be a party to the conflict and orders closure of Iraqi airspace to US combat missions. The order is partially unenforceable as US forces maintain operational control over portions of Iraqi airspace. The declaration creates a legal and diplomatic crisis as US strikes on PMF targets violate the stated neutrality.
timeline_source: Iraqi PM office / AP / Al Jazeera

event_description: Strait of Hormuz effectively closed: Iran deploys naval mines and activates anti-ship missile batteries along the Strait of Hormuz. IRGC Navy fast attack craft patrol the waterway. Commercial shipping diverts. Lloyd's of London suspends Gulf transit insurance.
timeline_data_point: 20% of global oil supply route closed

event_description: Treasury yields plunge as investors flee to safety: 10-year US Treasury yield drops 28 basis points to 3.92% as global investors flood into US government bonds. The dollar index (DXY) surges 2.1% to 107.8 on safe-haven demand. Gold spikes to $4,850/oz. The flight to safety is the largest single-day move since the early days of COVID-19.

S&P 500 drops 3.1% — worst day since 2024: US stock markets sell off sharply as the full scope of Operation Epic Fury becomes clear. S&P 500 closes at 6,654, down 3.1%. Nasdaq falls 3.8% on tech exposure to global supply chains. Defense stocks (Lockheed Martin, Raytheon, Northrop Grumman) surge 8-12%. Airlines plunge 11%.

Oil surges further as Hormuz closure confirmed: Brent crude closes at $89.20/bbl (+7.2% from yesterday's already elevated close) as markets price in the Strait of Hormuz closure. WTI at $85.40. The combined loss of Iranian exports and Hormuz transit creates a theoretical shortfall of 12+ million barrels per day — the largest supply disruption in oil market history.

HMS Anson (S123) — DEPLOYED — UK submarine supporting coalition operations in Arabian Sea
strike_notes: Day 29: Royal Navy Astute-class SSN. Departed Perth March 6. Tomahawk-armed. Northern Arabian Sea (reported). No official UK confirmation of exact position.

event_description: Pentagon confirms strikes on all known Iranian nuclear sites: DoD releases initial battle damage assessment confirming strikes on Natanz (centrifuge facility), Fordow (underground enrichment site), Isfahan (uranium conversion), and the Arak heavy water reactor. Natanz surface facilities assessed as destroyed. Fordow, buried under 80+ meters of rock, sustained multiple GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrator hits — damage assessment ongoing. Isfahan conversion facility severely damaged. Arak reactor building collapsed.
timeline_data_point: 4 nuclear sites struck — Fordow penetration uncertain

event_description: Iran's integrated air defense network assessed as suppressed: CENTCOM assesses Iran's integrated air defense system as effectively suppressed after 48 hours. S-300PMU-2 batteries at Isfahan and Tehran are confirmed destroyed. Indigenous Bavar-373 systems engaged but failed to intercept US stealth aircraft. Iranian Air Force F-14 fleet did not launch; their Mehrabad hangars were struck on Day 1. Remaining mobile SAMs and MANPADS continue to pose threats to non-stealth aircraft.
timeline_data_point: Iranian IADS suppressed within 48 hours

event_description: Houthis declare war on US and Israeli shipping in Red Sea: Ansar Allah (Houthi) military spokesman Yahya Saree announces all US and Israeli-linked vessels transiting the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb Strait are legitimate targets. The declaration extends the Houthis' existing Red Sea campaign from the Gaza war era to the new conflict.
timeline_source: Ansar Allah media / Al Masirah TV

event_description: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
location_name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
strike_notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

event_description: Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar: Shahab-3 MRBM / cruise missiles — Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
strike_notes: CENTCOM forward HQ. Qatar Defence Ministry said it thwarted attacks per pre-approved security plan. Two missiles reached base on March 1 per Qatar's official news agency. Long-range early warning radar in northern Qatar also targeted. Dozens of explosions heard in Doha from Patriot intercepts.

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Erbil, Iraq: Ballistic missiles — 1 French chief warrant officer KIA. Several French soldiers wounded. 1 airport guard killed. Iraqi Kurdish casualties reported. 27 total killed in Iraq (including 21 pro-Iran faction fighters, 3 Iranian Kurdish fighters, 1 civilian near Baghdad).
casualties_reported: 1 French chief warrant officer KIA. Several French soldiers wounded. 1 airport guard killed. Iraqi Kurdish casualties reported. 27 total killed in Iraq (including 21 pro-Iran faction fighters, 3 Iranian Kurdish fighters, 1 civilian near Baghdad).
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): 6 Kurdish Peshmerga killed, 30 wounded in Iranian ballistic missile strikes north of Erbil. Cumulative: US consulate area, airport targeted. French soldier KIA. Kurdish Peshmerga positions targeted.

event_description: Ain al-Asad Air Base, Iraq: Ballistic missiles / rockets — Minor injuries
strike_notes: Repeat target from 2020 Iranian retaliation. US forces dispersed after lessons learned. [NOTE: Limited specific sourcing for Ain al-Asad during 2026 conflict. Entry based on pattern of attacks on US facilities across Iraq and confirmed IRGC claims of striking 27+ bases. Specific active_days and casualty details require further verification.]

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
location_name: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
casualties_reported: 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
strike_notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

event_description: Beit Shemesh, Israel: Iranian ballistic missile — 9 killed (including 3 teenage siblings), 49 wounded, 11 missing
strike_notes: Deadliest single strike on Israeli civilians in the conflict. Iranian ballistic missile struck synagogue and public bomb shelter in residential neighborhood, 35km west of Jerusalem. Roof of reinforced shelter collapsed. Two interceptors had been launched against the missile but failed. Demonstrated limits of Israeli missile defense even for reinforced shelters. Three Bitton siblings (ages 16, 15, 13) among the dead. United Hatzalah volunteer Ronit Elimelech and her mother Sarah also killed.

event_description: Port Shuaiba, Kuwait: Drone / projectile (under investigation) — 6 US Army Reserve soldiers KIA, multiple wounded
casualties_reported: 6 US Army Reserve soldiers KIA, multiple wounded
strike_notes: Deadliest single attack on US forces in the conflict. Direct hit on makeshift tactical operations center (triple-wide trailer) at civilian port, ~9am local time March 1. One projectile penetrated air defenses. All 6 KIA were from 103rd Sustainment Command (Army Reserve, Des Moines, Iowa): Capt. Cody Khork (35), SFC Nicole Amor (39), SFC Noah Tietjens (42), Sgt. Declan Coady (20), Maj. Jeffrey O'Brien, CWO3 Robert Marzan. Bodies of two initially unaccounted for, recovered from burning building. Dignified transfer at Dover AFB attended by President Trump on March 8.

event_description: Camp Buehring, Kuwait: Drones — 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
strike_notes: Northeastern Kuwait. Iranian drone struck the US garrison. Maj. Davius died during medical emergency at Camp Buehring on March 6 — incident under investigation, classified as health-related, not combat. IRGC claimed 230-drone wave targeting Camp Buehring among other Kuwait facilities on Day 6.

event_description: Jordan (multiple locations): Ballistic missiles / drones — 14 people injured
strike_notes: Jordan intercepted 2 ballistic missiles on Feb 28. Total of 119 Iranian missiles and drones targeted Jordan. 14 people injured. Jordan has historically cooperated with US/Israeli air defense operations.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Isfahan: Natanz uranium enrichment facility
strike_notes: Primary nuclear targets struck in first wave. Deep penetrating munitions used. Day 22 (Mar 21): Natanz Shahid Ahmadi-Roshan Enrichment Complex struck with bunker-buster bombs (likely GBU-57 MOPs and/or GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrators). Israel denied involvement ('did not conduct any strikes in the area'). IAEA: 'No increase in off-site radiation levels.' DG Grossi called for 'military restraint to avoid any risk of nuclear accident.' Russia condemned as 'blatant violation of international law.' Entrance buildings of underground Fuel Enrichment Plant damaged. Bulk of Iran's enriched uranium (~440 kg) believed at Isfahan, not Natanz. Third strike on Natanz (after June 2025 Operation Midnight Hammer and early March 2026).

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

strike_notes: Multiple military targets struck across Ahvaz over the course of the war. Aboozar Children's Hospital in Ahvaz reported damaged (not directly struck) as early as March 2 per Iranian authorities and Al Jazeera. IDF announced March 13 strikes on 'headquarters of various regime bodies' in Ahvaz simultaneously with Tehran and Shiraz. Iranian Deputy Health Minister Ali Jafarian reported 31 major clinical facilities and hospitals damaged nationwide, 12 rendered inactive, as of Day 14. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran.

event_description: Karaj: Military and government sites (Day 1 target city)
strike_notes: Listed alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Qom, and Kermanshah as a Day 1 target city. Western Tehran suburb with significant military infrastructure. Local accounts on Day 13 described ~20 explosions in Shahriar, ~30 in Malard/Fardis, 7-8 in Garmdareh.

event_description: Karaj: Continued strikes on Karaj, Fardis, Malard, Garmdareh suburbs in subsequent days
strike_notes: Listed alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Qom, and Kermanshah as a Day 1 target city. Western Tehran suburb with significant military infrastructure. Local accounts on Day 13 described ~20 explosions in Shahriar, ~30 in Malard/Fardis, 7-8 in Garmdareh.

event_description: Qom: Military and government sites in Qom city proper (distinct from Fordow enrichment facility)
strike_notes: Qom city listed as a Day 1 target alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Karaj, Kermanshah, and Kerman. Fordow enrichment facility (near Qom) tracked separately. Continued strikes reported in subsequent days.

event_description: Konarak: Konarak Naval Base
strike_notes: Southern coastal naval base. Confirmed struck by satellite imagery published by USNI News (March 1 image). Located in Sistan-Baluchistan province, 400km southeast of Minab. Part of US southern flank naval suppression campaign (Operation Epic Fury).

event_description: Khojir (Tehran Province): Structures flanked by earthen berms (weapons/munitions research)
strike_notes: Military complex east of Tehran. Satellite imagery captured March 7 confirmed several structures destroyed. Long associated with weapons research programs.

event_description: Khojir (Tehran Province): Underground facilities
strike_notes: Military complex east of Tehran. Satellite imagery captured March 7 confirmed several structures destroyed. Long associated with weapons research programs.

event_description: Isfahan (Missile Complex): Isfahan Missile Complex — underground facilities
strike_notes: Distinct from Isfahan nuclear facilities (tracked under isfahan-nuclear). Ground-penetrating munitions used on underground missile silos. IDF confirmed on March 8 that IAF strikes on March 7 destroyed F-14 fighter jets at an airbase in Isfahan, likely the 8th Tactical Airbase.

event_description: Isfahan (Missile Complex): Artesh Air Force 8th Tactical Airbase (F-14 jets destroyed, confirmed by IDF Mar 7)
strike_notes: Distinct from Isfahan nuclear facilities (tracked under isfahan-nuclear). Ground-penetrating munitions used on underground missile silos. IDF confirmed on March 8 that IAF strikes on March 7 destroyed F-14 fighter jets at an airbase in Isfahan, likely the 8th Tactical Airbase.

event_description: India scrambles to evacuate 8 million citizens from Gulf states: India launches Operation Kaveri II — a massive diplomatic and logistics effort to prepare evacuation of up to 8 million Indian nationals across Gulf states. Indian Navy deploys three ships to the Arabian Sea. India calls for 'immediate de-escalation' but declines to condemn either side, seeking to maintain its oil supply relationship with both the US and Iran.
timeline_data_point: 8 million Indian nationals in Gulf states

event_description: Erdogan offers to mediate — condemns US strikes: Turkish President Erdogan condemns the US strikes as 'unilateral aggression that destabilizes the entire region' while simultaneously offering Ankara as a venue for negotiations. Turkey closes its airspace to US combat operations but continues allowing NATO surveillance flights. Turkey's position is complicated by its NATO membership, its economic ties to Iran, and the Kurdish factor.
Gas prices begin climbing across US: National average gas price rises to $3.25/gallon, up from $2.98 pre-war. Analysts project $4+ within weeks if Hormuz remains closed.
timeline_data_point: Gas +$0.27/gal in 48 hours

event_description: Day 3 estimated killed — Iran (military): 122. Context: Day 3 — Hezbollah enters; Strait closure; friendly fire incident
Day 3 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 15. Context: Day 3 — Hezbollah enters; Strait closure; friendly fire incident
Day 3 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 3 — Hezbollah enters; Strait closure; friendly fire incident
Day 3 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 44. Context: Day 3 — Hezbollah enters; Strait closure; friendly fire incident
Day 3 estimated killed — Israel (military): 0. Context: Day 3 — Hezbollah enters; Strait closure; friendly fire incident

Second wave of militia attacks on US forces in Iraq: Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq claims responsibility for drone attacks on Camp Victory near Baghdad International Airport and a logistics convoy on Route Tampa. One US contractor killed. Iraqi government calls for 'restraint by all parties' but does not condemn the attacks.
timeline_data_point: 1 US contractor KIA

event_description: Hezbollah enters the war: Hezbollah launches massive rocket and missile barrage on northern Israel. Radwan Force units attempt cross-border incursion at Metula. IDF responds with airstrikes across Lebanon.
timeline_data_point: 500+ rockets launched at Israel in first 12 hours

event_description: 3 Israeli civilians killed in Hezbollah rocket barrage on Haifa: Three Israeli civilians killed and 22 wounded when a Hezbollah rocket barrage overwhelms Iron Dome coverage over Haifa. The volume of simultaneous launches — 200+ in a 10-minute window targeting northern Israel — exceeds the system's engagement capacity in the Haifa sector. IDF calls up additional reserve battalions.
timeline_data_point: 3 Israeli civilians KIA, 22 wounded

event_description: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
location_name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
strike_notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

event_description: Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar: Shahab-3 MRBM / cruise missiles — Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
strike_notes: CENTCOM forward HQ. Qatar Defence Ministry said it thwarted attacks per pre-approved security plan. Two missiles reached base on March 1 per Qatar's official news agency. Long-range early warning radar in northern Qatar also targeted. Dozens of explosions heard in Doha from Patriot intercepts.

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Erbil, Iraq: Ballistic missiles — 1 French chief warrant officer KIA. Several French soldiers wounded. 1 airport guard killed. Iraqi Kurdish casualties reported. 27 total killed in Iraq (including 21 pro-Iran faction fighters, 3 Iranian Kurdish fighters, 1 civilian near Baghdad).
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): 6 Kurdish Peshmerga killed, 30 wounded in Iranian ballistic missile strikes north of Erbil. Cumulative: US consulate area, airport targeted. French soldier KIA. Kurdish Peshmerga positions targeted.

event_description: Ain al-Asad Air Base, Iraq: Ballistic missiles / rockets — Minor injuries
strike_notes: Repeat target from 2020 Iranian retaliation. US forces dispersed after lessons learned. [NOTE: Limited specific sourcing for Ain al-Asad during 2026 conflict. Entry based on pattern of attacks on US facilities across Iraq and confirmed IRGC claims of striking 27+ bases. Specific active_days and casualty details require further verification.]

event_description: Haifa, Israel: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified) — Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
weapon_system: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified)
casualties_reported: Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
strike_notes: Hezbollah targeted missile defense site south of Haifa on March 2, first strikes since 2024 ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched drone swarm at Ramat David airbase targeting radar sites and control rooms. Direct Iranian missile fire at Haifa unverified — most Haifa-area attacks attributed to Hezbollah.

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
casualties_reported: 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Ras Tanura, Saudi Arabia: Cruise missiles / drones (Houthi) — Infrastructure damage — no casualties reported
strike_notes: World's largest oil loading facility. Saudi Arabia confirmed shooting down two drones, debris started limited fire. No civilian injuries. Saudi Aramco warned of 'catastrophic consequences' for oil markets. Saudi Arabia increasingly diverted oil to Red Sea port of Yanbu via East-West Crude Oil Pipeline.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
casualties_reported: 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
location_name: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
strike_notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

event_description: Camp Buehring, Kuwait: Drones — 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
strike_notes: Northeastern Kuwait. Iranian drone struck the US garrison. Maj. Davius died during medical emergency at Camp Buehring on March 6 — incident under investigation, classified as health-related, not combat. IRGC claimed 230-drone wave targeting Camp Buehring among other Kuwait facilities on Day 6.

event_description: RAF Akrotiri, Cyprus: Drone — No casualties reported
strike_notes: British air force base on Mediterranean island of Cyprus struck by Iranian drone. No casualties. Represents furthest west Iranian retaliation reached. UK sovereign base area.

event_description: US Embassy, Hawalli, Kuwait: Missile (type unconfirmed) — Unknown — embassy personnel sheltering in place
casualties_reported: Unknown — embassy personnel sheltering in place
strike_notes: US Embassy in Kuwait likely hit by Iranian missile strike per Wikipedia citing multiple sources. Xinhua published screenshot showing smoke billowing near embassy area on March 2. US Embassy warned public not to approach, all personnel sheltering in place. US embassies in Saudi Arabia and UAE consulate in Dubai also reportedly targeted by drones during the week.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Isfahan: Natanz uranium enrichment facility
strike_notes: Primary nuclear targets struck in first wave. Deep penetrating munitions used. Day 22 (Mar 21): Natanz Shahid Ahmadi-Roshan Enrichment Complex struck with bunker-buster bombs (likely GBU-57 MOPs and/or GBU-72 5,000-lb penetrators). Israel denied involvement ('did not conduct any strikes in the area'). IAEA: 'No increase in off-site radiation levels.' DG Grossi called for 'military restraint to avoid any risk of nuclear accident.' Russia condemned as 'blatant violation of international law.' Entrance buildings of underground Fuel Enrichment Plant damaged. Bulk of Iran's enriched uranium (~440 kg) believed at Isfahan, not Natanz. Third strike on Natanz (after June 2025 Operation Midnight Hammer and early March 2026).

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

strike_notes: Southern coastal naval base. Confirmed struck by satellite imagery published by USNI News (March 1 image). Located in Sistan-Baluchistan province, 400km southeast of Minab. Part of US southern flank naval suppression campaign (Operation Epic Fury).

strike_notes: Distinct from Isfahan nuclear facilities (tracked under isfahan-nuclear). Ground-penetrating munitions used on underground missile silos. IDF confirmed on March 8 that IAF strikes on March 7 destroyed F-14 fighter jets at an airbase in Isfahan, likely the 8th Tactical Airbase.

event_description: Isfahan (Missile Complex): Artesh Air Force 8th Tactical Airbase (F-14 jets destroyed, confirmed by IDF Mar 7)

event_description: US Cyber Command disrupts Iranian military communications: US Cyber Command confirms offensive operations degrading IRGC command-and-control networks and Iranian military communications infrastructure. Operations are credited with slowing Iran's ability to coordinate retaliatory missile strikes. Details remain classified.

Japan and South Korea announce coordinated SPR releases: Japan and South Korea, both heavily dependent on Persian Gulf oil, announce coordinated Strategic Petroleum Reserve releases of 15 million and 7 million barrels respectively. Both governments support the US position but urge rapid de-escalation. Japan dispatches Maritime Self-Defense Force vessels to support Indian Ocean shipping escort operations.
timeline_source: Japanese PM office / South Korean MFA / Reuters
timeline_data_point: 22 million barrels combined SPR release

event_description: Markets stabilize as investors assess conflict duration: S&P 500 closes at 6,612, down modestly. Markets begin differentiating between sectors — defense and energy surge while airlines, travel, and consumer discretionary continue declining. Gold breaks $4,900/oz as safe-haven demand accelerates.

Brent crude hits $91.50 — third straight day of gains: Oil prices continue climbing as Hezbollah's entry and Houthi Red Sea escalation widen the conflict's geographic footprint. Brent closes at $91.50. Saudi Arabia and UAE pledge to increase production from non-Hormuz export terminals but capacity is limited to approximately 2 MBD via pipeline to Red Sea ports.
timeline_data_point: Brent $91.50. Saudi pledges 2 MBD via alternate routes

event_description: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
location_name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
strike_notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

event_description: Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar: Shahab-3 MRBM / cruise missiles — Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
strike_notes: CENTCOM forward HQ. Qatar Defence Ministry said it thwarted attacks per pre-approved security plan. Two missiles reached base on March 1 per Qatar's official news agency. Long-range early warning radar in northern Qatar also targeted. Dozens of explosions heard in Doha from Patriot intercepts.

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Ain al-Asad Air Base, Iraq: Ballistic missiles / rockets — Minor injuries
strike_notes: Repeat target from 2020 Iranian retaliation. US forces dispersed after lessons learned. [NOTE: Limited specific sourcing for Ain al-Asad during 2026 conflict. Entry based on pattern of attacks on US facilities across Iraq and confirmed IRGC claims of striking 27+ bases. Specific active_days and casualty details require further verification.]

event_description: Haifa, Israel: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified) — Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
weapon_system: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified)
strike_notes: Hezbollah targeted missile defense site south of Haifa on March 2, first strikes since 2024 ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched drone swarm at Ramat David airbase targeting radar sites and control rooms. Direct Iranian missile fire at Haifa unverified — most Haifa-area attacks attributed to Hezbollah.

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Ras Tanura, Saudi Arabia: Cruise missiles / drones (Houthi) — Infrastructure damage — no casualties reported
strike_notes: World's largest oil loading facility. Saudi Arabia confirmed shooting down two drones, debris started limited fire. No civilian injuries. Saudi Aramco warned of 'catastrophic consequences' for oil markets. Saudi Arabia increasingly diverted oil to Red Sea port of Yanbu via East-West Crude Oil Pipeline.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Camp Buehring, Kuwait: Drones — 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
strike_notes: Northeastern Kuwait. Iranian drone struck the US garrison. Maj. Davius died during medical emergency at Camp Buehring on March 6 — incident under investigation, classified as health-related, not combat. IRGC claimed 230-drone wave targeting Camp Buehring among other Kuwait facilities on Day 6.

event_description: US Embassy, Hawalli, Kuwait: Missile (type unconfirmed) — Unknown — embassy personnel sheltering in place
strike_notes: US Embassy in Kuwait likely hit by Iranian missile strike per Wikipedia citing multiple sources. Xinhua published screenshot showing smoke billowing near embassy area on March 2. US Embassy warned public not to approach, all personnel sheltering in place. US embassies in Saudi Arabia and UAE consulate in Dubai also reportedly targeted by drones during the week.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base

Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

timeline_data_point: 2M+ global protesters

Satellite imagery shows Fordow survived deep strikes: Commercial satellite imagery from Planet Labs and analysis by the Institute for Science and International Security show that while Fordow's surface buildings and tunnel entrances are destroyed, thermal signatures suggest underground centrifuge halls may be partially intact. The site's depth exceeds the penetration capability of existing US bunker busters. Critics call this a 'strategic failure at the war's central objective.'
timeline_source: ISIS / Planet Labs / Arms Control Association
timeline_data_point: Fordow underground halls likely survived

event_description: Drone swarm overwhelms air defenses at Al Udeid, Qatar: A coordinated salvo of 40+ Shahed drones and 5 ballistic missiles targets Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar — the largest US air base in the Middle East. Most are intercepted but 3 drones penetrate defenses and strike the airfield, damaging a KC-135 tanker aircraft. No US fatalities. The incident raises questions about interceptor stockpile sustainability.
timeline_data_point: 3 drones penetrate Al Udeid defenses

event_description: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
location_name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
strike_notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

event_description: Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar: Shahab-3 MRBM / cruise missiles — Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
strike_notes: CENTCOM forward HQ. Qatar Defence Ministry said it thwarted attacks per pre-approved security plan. Two missiles reached base on March 1 per Qatar's official news agency. Long-range early warning radar in northern Qatar also targeted. Dozens of explosions heard in Doha from Patriot intercepts.

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Haifa, Israel: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified) — Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
weapon_system: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified)
strike_notes: Hezbollah targeted missile defense site south of Haifa on March 2, first strikes since 2024 ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched drone swarm at Ramat David airbase targeting radar sites and control rooms. Direct Iranian missile fire at Haifa unverified — most Haifa-area attacks attributed to Hezbollah.

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Ras Tanura, Saudi Arabia: Cruise missiles / drones (Houthi) — Infrastructure damage — no casualties reported
strike_notes: World's largest oil loading facility. Saudi Arabia confirmed shooting down two drones, debris started limited fire. No civilian injuries. Saudi Aramco warned of 'catastrophic consequences' for oil markets. Saudi Arabia increasingly diverted oil to Red Sea port of Yanbu via East-West Crude Oil Pipeline.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Camp Buehring, Kuwait: Drones — 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
strike_notes: Northeastern Kuwait. Iranian drone struck the US garrison. Maj. Davius died during medical emergency at Camp Buehring on March 6 — incident under investigation, classified as health-related, not combat. IRGC claimed 230-drone wave targeting Camp Buehring among other Kuwait facilities on Day 6.

event_description: US Embassy, Hawalli, Kuwait: Missile (type unconfirmed) — Unknown — embassy personnel sheltering in place
strike_notes: US Embassy in Kuwait likely hit by Iranian missile strike per Wikipedia citing multiple sources. Xinhua published screenshot showing smoke billowing near embassy area on March 2. US Embassy warned public not to approach, all personnel sheltering in place. US embassies in Saudi Arabia and UAE consulate in Dubai also reportedly targeted by drones during the week.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.
