event_description: First polls show narrow majority supports strikes — but opposition to ground war: An NBC/WSJ flash poll shows 54% of Americans support the initial strikes on Iran's nuclear facilities, but 71% oppose any ground invasion. Support breaks sharply along partisan lines — 82% of Republicans approve vs. 31% of Democrats. The school strike in Minab has not yet been widely covered at the time of polling.
timeline_data_point: 54% approve strikes, 71% oppose ground war

event_description: S&P 500 enters correction territory: US stock markets fall sharply as conflict escalation fears mount. S&P 500 drops to 6,297 — down 8.3% from pre-war close of 6,878.88. Defense stocks surge; airlines collapse. VIX spikes above 35.
timeline_data_point: S&P 500 6,297 — down 8.5% from pre-war

event_description: 14,000+ flights cancelled across Middle East and Europe: Airspace closures over Iran, Iraq, and parts of the Persian Gulf force massive flight cancellations. European carriers reroute around conflict zone, adding hours and fuel costs.
timeline_data_point: 14,000+ flights cancelled

event_description: Oil breaks $95 as conflict shows no signs of de-escalation: Brent closes at $95.30/bbl on the back of continued Hormuz closure, Houthi Red Sea attacks, and depletion of global crude inventories. IEA releases emergency assessment estimating commercial inventories can cover the shortfall for 45-60 days before critical shortages emerge in Asia-Pacific and European markets.
timeline_data_point: Brent $95.30. IEA: 45-60 day inventory runway

event_description: Day 6 estimated killed — Iran (military): 82. Context: Day 6 — 500+ ballistic missiles fired; Bapco refinery hit
Day 6 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 20. Context: Day 6 — 500+ ballistic missiles fired; Bapco refinery hit
Day 6 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 6 — 500+ ballistic missiles fired; Bapco refinery hit
Day 6 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 68. Context: Day 6 — 500+ ballistic missiles fired; Bapco refinery hit
Day 6 estimated killed — Israel (military): 0. Context: Day 6 — 500+ ballistic missiles fired; Bapco refinery hit

US strikes PMF weapons depots in Iraq: US conducts airstrikes on three Kata'ib Hezbollah weapons storage facilities in Jurf al-Sakhar and near the Iraq-Syria border. Iraqi Prime Minister condemns the strikes as a 'violation of sovereignty.' Iraqi parliament begins emergency session to discuss expelling US forces.
timeline_data_point: 3 PMF sites struck on Iraqi soil

event_description: Houthis strike container ship in Red Sea — crew evacuated: A Greek-owned container vessel is struck by two Houthi anti-ship ballistic missiles approximately 60 nautical miles southwest of Al Hudaydah. The ship sustains significant damage and the crew is evacuated. Maersk and MSC announce suspension of all Red Sea transits, rerouting around the Cape of Good Hope.
timeline_data_point: Major shipping lines suspend Red Sea transits

event_description: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
location_name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
strike_notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

event_description: Al Udeid Air Base, Qatar: Shahab-3 MRBM / cruise missiles — Two missiles reached base on March 1. Early warning radar targeted. No US casualties reported.
strike_notes: CENTCOM forward HQ. Qatar Defence Ministry said it thwarted attacks per pre-approved security plan. Two missiles reached base on March 1 per Qatar's official news agency. Long-range early warning radar in northern Qatar also targeted. Dozens of explosions heard in Doha from Patriot intercepts.

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Camp Buehring, Kuwait: Drones — 1 US National Guard soldier died of health-related incident on March 6 (Maj. Sorffly Davius, 46, NYPD officer). Not classified as combat death.
strike_notes: Northeastern Kuwait. Iranian drone struck the US garrison. Maj. Davius died during medical emergency at Camp Buehring on March 6 — incident under investigation, classified as health-related, not combat. IRGC claimed 230-drone wave targeting Camp Buehring among other Kuwait facilities on Day 6.

event_description: Duqm / Salalah, Oman: Drones — At least 1 fuel storage tank damaged in Duqm
casualties_reported: At least 1 fuel storage tank damaged in Duqm
strike_notes: Oman's deep-water ports (Duqm, Salalah, Sohar) are potential bypass routes around Strait of Hormuz closure. Drone strikes on Duqm and Salalah damaged fuel infrastructure and discouraged alternative routing. Sohar fell within insurer war risk area. Oman was only GCC country Iran did not strike on Day 1, given its mediator role. These strikes represent escalation against a traditional neutral party. First_strike date approximate — verify against maritime intelligence dailies.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

event_description: Karaj: Military and government sites (Day 1 target city)
strike_notes: Listed alongside Tehran, Isfahan, Qom, and Kermanshah as a Day 1 target city. Western Tehran suburb with significant military infrastructure. Local accounts on Day 13 described ~20 explosions in Shahriar, ~30 in Malard/Fardis, 7-8 in Garmdareh.

event_description: Karaj: Continued strikes on Karaj, Fardis, Malard, Garmdareh suburbs in subsequent days

event_description: Khojir (Tehran Province): Structures flanked by earthen berms (weapons/munitions research)
strike_notes: Military complex east of Tehran. Satellite imagery captured March 7 confirmed several structures destroyed. Long associated with weapons research programs.

event_description: Khojir (Tehran Province): Underground facilities
strike_notes: Military complex east of Tehran. Satellite imagery captured March 7 confirmed several structures destroyed. Long associated with weapons research programs.

event_description: Tehran: Golestan Palace complex (UNESCO World Heritage Site — damaged by strike on Arg Square, Mar 2)
casualties_reported: Multiple incidents — see individual notes; 460+ killed in Tehran alone as of Day 11
strike_notes: Aggregated entry for confirmed civilian infrastructure damage across Tehran beyond the dedicated residential and military entries. Iranian MFA claimed 33 civilian locations struck nationwide including hospitals, schools, residential areas, the Grand Bazaar, Golestan Palace, and Azadi Stadium. Iranian Red Crescent reported 3,600+ civilian sites damaged as of Day 6; 20,000 civilian buildings including 16,000 residential units affected as of Day 11. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran (4 healthcare workers killed, 25 injured, 4 ambulances affected). 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent; 31 major clinical facilities/hospitals damaged and 12 rendered inactive per Deputy Health Minister. Niloofar Square strike (Day 3) killed 20 civilians per state media.

event_description: Oman begins quiet shuttle diplomacy between Washington and Tehran: Oman's Sultan Haitham initiates back-channel communications between US and Iranian officials — reprising Oman's traditional mediator role. Swiss Ambassador to Iran (US protecting power) facilitates initial contact. Discussions are preliminary and focused on humanitarian corridors and POW treatment rather than ceasefire terms. Both sides deny talks are taking place.
timeline_source: Diplomatic sources / Omani media / Reuters (sourced anonymously)

event_description: 450,000 displaced in Lebanon: UNHCR reports 450,000 people displaced in Lebanon as Israeli strikes intensify against Hezbollah positions. Refugee flows toward Syria and northern Lebanon overwhelm capacity.
timeline_data_point: 450,000 displaced

event_description: Iranian civilian death toll estimated at 400+: Iranian Red Crescent and state media report over 400 civilian deaths from US and Israeli strikes in the first week. Independent verification is impossible due to media access restrictions. Iran claims the number is significantly higher. Pentagon states all targets are military or dual-use and that civilian casualties are 'deeply regretted but not the objective.'
timeline_data_point: 400+ Iranian civilian deaths (Iranian figures)

event_description: Day 7 estimated killed — Iran (military): 73. Context: Day 7 — One week; reduced tempo; tugboat sunk (3 crew missing)
Day 7 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 15. Context: Day 7 — One week; reduced tempo; tugboat sunk (3 crew missing)
Day 7 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 7 — One week; reduced tempo; tugboat sunk (3 crew missing)
Day 7 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 61. Context: Day 7 — One week; reduced tempo; tugboat sunk (3 crew missing)
Day 7 estimated killed — Israel (military): 0. Context: Day 7 — One week; reduced tempo; tugboat sunk (3 crew missing)

7th US service member killed — drone attack in Syria: A US Army soldier is killed by a one-way attack drone at a patrol base in northeastern Syria. The drone, attributed to an Iranian-aligned militia, struck a housing unit. This is the 7th US combat death of the conflict and the first outside the Persian Gulf theater.
timeline_data_point: 7 US KIA total

event_description: Iron Dome interceptor stockpile concerns emerge: Israeli media reports the IDF has expended over 1,500 Iron Dome and David's Sling interceptors in the first week of conflict — a rate that strains production capacity. The US expedites delivery of Tamir interceptors from existing stocks. A senior IDF officer warns off-record that at current rates, 'we have weeks, not months' of interceptor supply for the northern front.
timeline_source: IDF (background briefing) / Haaretz / Times of Israel
timeline_data_point: 1,500+ interceptors expended in 7 days

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

event_description: Tehran: Golestan Palace complex (UNESCO World Heritage Site — damaged by strike on Arg Square, Mar 2)
casualties_reported: Multiple incidents — see individual notes; 460+ killed in Tehran alone as of Day 11
strike_notes: Aggregated entry for confirmed civilian infrastructure damage across Tehran beyond the dedicated residential and military entries. Iranian MFA claimed 33 civilian locations struck nationwide including hospitals, schools, residential areas, the Grand Bazaar, Golestan Palace, and Azadi Stadium. Iranian Red Crescent reported 3,600+ civilian sites damaged as of Day 6; 20,000 civilian buildings including 16,000 residential units affected as of Day 11. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran (4 healthcare workers killed, 25 injured, 4 ambulances affected). 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent; 31 major clinical facilities/hospitals damaged and 12 rendered inactive per Deputy Health Minister. Niloofar Square strike (Day 3) killed 20 civilians per state media.

strike_notes: Distinct from Isfahan nuclear facilities (tracked under isfahan-nuclear). Ground-penetrating munitions used on underground missile silos. IDF confirmed on March 8 that IAF strikes on March 7 destroyed F-14 fighter jets at an airbase in Isfahan, likely the 8th Tactical Airbase.

event_description: Isfahan (Missile Complex): Artesh Air Force 8th Tactical Airbase (F-14 jets destroyed, confirmed by IDF Mar 7)

event_description: Iranian hackers deface US government websites and leak military emails: An Iranian hacking group calling itself 'Cyber Avengers' defaces several US municipal government websites and leaks a cache of emails allegedly from CENTCOM personnel. Pentagon states leaked materials are mostly unclassified and some appear fabricated. The information warfare dimension of the conflict intensifies.
Brent crude breaks $100/barrel: Oil strikes push Brent past the $100 psychological barrier for the first time since 2022. WTI at $97. Analysts warn of $120+ if conflict extends beyond 30 days.
timeline_data_point: Brent $101.50 — first time above $100 since 2022

event_description: Markets rebound on SPR release and limited ground war signals: S&P 500 recovers to 6,580 as the SPR release announcement and White House confirmation of 'no ground troops' provide modest reassurance. The rebound is driven by short-covering and bargain hunting in oversold tech names. Analysts warn the recovery is fragile and contingent on no further escalation.
timeline_data_point: S&P 6,580 (+4.5% from Mar 5 low)

event_description: Day 8 estimated killed — Iran (military): 114. Context: Day 8 — Oil infrastructure strikes begin; Brent approaches $100
Day 8 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 25. Context: Day 8 — Oil infrastructure strikes begin; Brent approaches $100

US strikes Iranian oil infrastructure: In major escalation, US strikes Kharg Island oil terminal (90% of Iran's exports), Abadan refinery, and oil storage facilities. Stated objective: degrade Iran's ability to fund ongoing retaliation.
timeline_data_point: 90% of Iran's oil export capacity targeted

event_description: Week 1 summary — Operation Epic Fury by the numbers: After 7 days: 3,000+ targets struck in Iran. Kharg Island, Natanz, Isfahan, Arak confirmed destroyed. Fordow status uncertain. Strait of Hormuz closed. Hezbollah and Houthi fronts active. 7 US KIA. 3 Israeli civilians killed. 400+ Iranian civilian dead (unverified). 450,000 displaced in Lebanon. Oil at $101.50/bbl. S&P 500 at 6,580 (-4.3%). Gas $3.38/gal (+13.4%). Gold $4,980/oz.
timeline_data_point: Week 1 cumulative

event_description: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — Runway damage, debris injuries. 3 Kuwaiti soldiers slightly injured. Italian facilities damaged.
location_name: Ali Al Salem Air Base, Kuwait
strike_notes: 386th Air Expeditionary Wing hub. Kuwaiti air defenses intercepted initial ballistic missiles on Feb 28; debris fell on base. Satellite imagery confirmed damage to 12+ structures, aircraft shelters, and runway areas. Camp Canada (Canadian forces) bunkers also damaged. On March 2, Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15Es in friendly fire incident — all 6 crew ejected safely. Italian 51st Stormo personnel sheltered in bunkers.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
location_name: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
strike_notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

event_description: Kharg Island: Oil export terminal
strike_notes: 90% of Iran's oil exports flow through Kharg. Struck in second-week escalation. Israel bombed 30 additional oil depots on Day 10. Day 15 (Mar 13): US executed large-scale precision strike on 90+ military targets including naval mine storage, missile storage bunkers, air defense facility, naval base, airport control tower/runway, and helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately preserved. Trump warned of future strikes if Hormuz remains blocked. Trump called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' Day 16: Trump threatens to hit Kharg again 'just for fun.'

event_description: Ahvaz: Artesh Ground Forces 92nd Division
casualties_reported: Unknown — military targets; hospital collateral damage reported separately
strike_notes: Multiple military targets struck across Ahvaz over the course of the war. Aboozar Children's Hospital in Ahvaz reported damaged (not directly struck) as early as March 2 per Iranian authorities and Al Jazeera. IDF announced March 13 strikes on 'headquarters of various regime bodies' in Ahvaz simultaneously with Tehran and Shiraz. Iranian Deputy Health Minister Ali Jafarian reported 31 major clinical facilities and hospitals damaged nationwide, 12 rendered inactive, as of Day 14. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran.

event_description: Tehran: Golestan Palace complex (UNESCO World Heritage Site — damaged by strike on Arg Square, Mar 2)
casualties_reported: Multiple incidents — see individual notes; 460+ killed in Tehran alone as of Day 11
strike_notes: Aggregated entry for confirmed civilian infrastructure damage across Tehran beyond the dedicated residential and military entries. Iranian MFA claimed 33 civilian locations struck nationwide including hospitals, schools, residential areas, the Grand Bazaar, Golestan Palace, and Azadi Stadium. Iranian Red Crescent reported 3,600+ civilian sites damaged as of Day 6; 20,000 civilian buildings including 16,000 residential units affected as of Day 11. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran (4 healthcare workers killed, 25 injured, 4 ambulances affected). 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent; 31 major clinical facilities/hospitals damaged and 12 rendered inactive per Deputy Health Minister. Niloofar Square strike (Day 3) killed 20 civilians per state media.

event_description: Shiraz (South): Underground ballistic missile production and storage facility south of Shiraz
strike_notes: Separate from the shiraz-airbase entry which covers Day 1-2 strikes. Satellite imagery March 7 showed ground-penetrating munitions used on underground facilities at a missile base south of Shiraz — one of at least 25 Iranian medium-range ballistic missile bases. June 2025 strikes had caused only light above-ground damage. IDF announced on March 13 that underground missile production site in Shiraz was struck. NCRI reports heavy strikes on Shiraz from early March 13, including airport, control tower, Gulf Highway, Moharrab Street, and SaIran complex.

event_description: US anti-war protests grow — 500,000 march in Washington: The largest US anti-war demonstration since 2003 draws an estimated 500,000 people to the National Mall in Washington, D.C. Simultaneous protests in 120+ US cities. The Minab school strike images have become the defining visual of the anti-war movement. Counter-protests supporting the troops draw smaller but vocal crowds in several cities.
timeline_source: AP / National Park Service estimate / Reuters
timeline_data_point: 500,000 in D.C. — largest since 2003

event_description: Iraqi parliament votes to expel US forces — non-binding: Iraqi parliament passes non-binding resolution demanding withdrawal of US forces from Iraqi territory. The vote is largely symbolic — a near-identical resolution passed in January 2020 after the Soleimani strike and was never enforced. Iraqi PM declines to sign an executive order. Militia attacks on US bases continue at a rate of 2-3 per day.
timeline_data_point: Non-binding expulsion vote passes

event_description: Strategic Petroleum Reserve release authorized: President Trump authorizes release of 30 million barrels from the Strategic Petroleum Reserve over 60 days. Critics note this replaces less than 3 days of Hormuz flow.
timeline_data_point: 30M barrels authorized

US strikes Houthi missile sites in Yemen: US Navy and Air Force assets strike Houthi anti-ship missile launch sites, drone storage facilities, and radar installations in Sana'a, Hodeidah, and Dhamar governorates. Houthis vow retaliation and launch drone swarm toward Eilat, Israel within hours.
timeline_data_point: 12 Houthi sites struck

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
location_name: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
strike_notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: IAEA loses all monitoring access to Iranian nuclear sites: International Atomic Energy Agency Director General confirms total loss of monitoring and verification access across all Iranian nuclear facilities. Inspectors were evacuated before the strikes. Iran announced withdrawal from the NPT on March 2. IAEA states it 'cannot assess the current status of Iran's nuclear material inventory.' The war intended to prevent an Iranian bomb may have made verification of that goal impossible.
timeline_data_point: IAEA monitoring access: zero

event_description: Mojtaba Khamenei named Supreme Leader: Iran's Assembly of Experts names Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the killed Supreme Leader, as the new Supreme Leader. Mojtaba vows 'resistance until victory' and rejects any ceasefire.

Global supply chain disruption spreads beyond energy: Disruption from Hormuz closure and Red Sea rerouting cascades beyond energy markets. Petrochemical feedstock shortages force production cuts at European chemical plants. Semiconductor-grade neon (produced partly in the Middle East supply chain) faces spot shortages. The Baltic Dry Index spikes 35% as available shipping capacity is absorbed by longer routes. Auto manufacturers warn of production slowdowns within 2-3 weeks.
timeline_data_point: Baltic Dry Index +35%

US air campaign targets Iranian drone production and storage: In a dedicated strike package, US forces target Iran's drone production infrastructure including facilities in Isfahan, Kermanshah, and a suspected underground Shahed assembly plant near Karaj. Defense Intelligence Agency estimates Iran began the conflict with 3,000+ one-way attack drones in stockpile. An unknown number remain operational despite strikes.

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
location_name: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
strike_notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

strike_notes: Aggregated entry for confirmed civilian infrastructure damage across Tehran beyond the dedicated residential and military entries. Niloofar Square strike (Day 3) killed 20 civilians per state media.

event_description: Red Sea shipping insurance premiums triple: War risk insurance premiums for Red Sea and Gulf of Aden transits triple to 2.5% of cargo value — up from 0.8% pre-war. Combined with Hormuz closure, approximately 35% of global seaborne oil trade is now disrupted or rerouted. Freight rates for Asia-Europe routes surge 40%.
timeline_data_point: 35% of seaborne oil trade disrupted

event_description: University of Michigan consumer sentiment plunges: University of Michigan preliminary consumer sentiment index drops to 62.1, down from 71.8 — the steepest single-period decline since March 2020. The decline is driven by expectations of higher gas and grocery prices. Americans cite the war as the primary reason for economic pessimism. Grocery chains begin implementing 5-8% price increases on imported goods.
timeline_data_point: Consumer sentiment 62.1, down from 71.8

event_description: Iranian infrastructure damage assessment — power, water, fuel: The ICRC reports significant damage to Iranian civilian infrastructure including power generation (30-40% of national grid offline in western Iran), water treatment disruption in Ahvaz and Kermanshah, and fuel shortages as refinery capacity is degraded. Hospitals are operating on backup generators. Iran's road and rail network is severely disrupted by strikes on bridges and logistics hubs.
timeline_data_point: 30-40% of western Iran power grid offline

S&P 500 recovers to 6,694 — rebounding 6.3% from last week's 6,297 low on SPR release, no-ground-war pledges, and short-covering. Markets remain 2.5% below pre-war 6,878.88. VIX holds above 25. Gas national average at $3.48 and climbing. Estimated US operational cost: $500M/day.
timeline_data_point: Estimated total cost: $5B+

strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
location_name: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
strike_notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

event_description: Kharg Island: Oil export terminal
strike_notes: 90% of Iran's oil exports flow through Kharg. Struck in second-week escalation. Israel bombed 30 additional oil depots on Day 10. Day 15 (Mar 13): US executed large-scale precision strike on 90+ military targets including naval mine storage, missile storage bunkers, air defense facility, naval base, airport control tower/runway, and helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately preserved. Trump warned of future strikes if Hormuz remains blocked. Trump called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' Day 16: Trump threatens to hit Kharg again 'just for fun.'

event_description: Tehran (East — Resalat Square): Residential apartment buildings near Resalat Square, Narmak neighborhood
strike_notes: Strike hit at 1:00 AM on March 10 (during Ramadan, when shops were open late and people were gathering). Three residential buildings bombed simultaneously on the same street, plus a police building. One of the deadliest attacks on Tehran since the start of the war. Rescue crews searched rubble for survivors. Second major civilian casualty incident in Tehran after Niloofar Square (Day 3, 20 killed).

event_description: Tehran: Golestan Palace complex (UNESCO World Heritage Site — damaged by strike on Arg Square, Mar 2)
casualties_reported: Multiple incidents — see individual notes; 460+ killed in Tehran alone as of Day 11
strike_notes: Aggregated entry for confirmed civilian infrastructure damage across Tehran beyond the dedicated residential and military entries. Iranian MFA claimed 33 civilian locations struck nationwide including hospitals, schools, residential areas, the Grand Bazaar, Golestan Palace, and Azadi Stadium. Iranian Red Crescent reported 3,600+ civilian sites damaged as of Day 6; 20,000 civilian buildings including 16,000 residential units affected as of Day 11. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran (4 healthcare workers killed, 25 injured, 4 ambulances affected). 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent; 31 major clinical facilities/hospitals damaged and 12 rendered inactive per Deputy Health Minister. Niloofar Square strike (Day 3) killed 20 civilians per state media.

event_description: Shiraz (South): Underground ballistic missile production and storage facility south of Shiraz
strike_notes: Separate from the shiraz-airbase entry which covers Day 1-2 strikes. Satellite imagery March 7 showed ground-penetrating munitions used on underground facilities at a missile base south of Shiraz — one of at least 25 Iranian medium-range ballistic missile bases. June 2025 strikes had caused only light above-ground damage. IDF announced on March 13 that underground missile production site in Shiraz was struck. NCRI reports heavy strikes on Shiraz from early March 13, including airport, control tower, Gulf Highway, Moharrab Street, and SaIran complex.

event_description: Trump sends mixed signals on war's end: President Trump tells CBS News the war is 'pretty much complete' but later tells House Republicans 'we haven't won enough.' Demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran. Mixed messaging roils markets and confuses diplomatic channels.

Oil crashes after Energy Secretary's false Hormuz claim: Energy Secretary Chris Wright posts on social media that the US Navy has escorted a tanker through the Strait of Hormuz. The claim is false — no escort occurred. Brent crude plunges 11.3% to $87.80 before partially recovering. The incident raises questions about information discipline within the administration during wartime.
timeline_data_point: Brent -11.3% in single session

event_description: Gas prices hit $3.63/gallon despite oil retreat: National average gas price rises to $3.63/gallon even as crude prices retreat. AAA notes gas prices lag oil by 7-10 days. California hits $4.81/gal. Analysts project continued climb as refinery capacity remains constrained by regional disruption.
timeline_data_point: $3.63/gal — +21.8% since pre-war

event_description: Day 12 estimated killed — Iran (military): 78. Context: Day 12 — French soldier killed; KC-135 crash (6 US KIA); 30+ minelayers destroyed
Day 12 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 45. Context: Day 12 — French soldier killed; KC-135 crash (6 US KIA); 30+ minelayers destroyed
Day 12 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 12 — French soldier killed; KC-135 crash (6 US KIA); 30+ minelayers destroyed
Day 12 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 54. Context: Day 12 — French soldier killed; KC-135 crash (6 US KIA); 30+ minelayers destroyed
Day 12 estimated killed — Israel (military): 0. Context: Day 12 — French soldier killed; KC-135 crash (6 US KIA); 30+ minelayers destroyed

Largest militia barrage yet — 40+ rockets at Al-Asad: PMF-aligned groups launch the largest single barrage of the conflict at Al-Asad Air Base — over 40 rockets and 5 one-way attack drones. US air defenses intercept most, but several impact the airfield, damaging two MQ-9 Reapers on the ground. Two US service members wounded. CENTCOM warns of 'decisive response.'
timeline_data_point: 40+ rockets, 2 drones destroyed on ground

event_description: Three ships hit in Strait of Hormuz and Persian Gulf: UK Navy reports three vessels struck by suspected projectiles: Thai-flagged bulk carrier Mayuree Naree in the Strait off Oman, a container vessel west of Ras Al-Khaimah, and a bulk carrier northwest of Dubai. Attacks come after a 72-hour lull in maritime targeting.
timeline_data_point: 3 vessels struck in single day

event_description: US death toll rises to 8: Pentagon confirms an eighth US service member has died from injuries sustained during an Iranian missile attack on US troops stationed at Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia on March 1. Sgt. Benjamin Pennington had been in critical condition since Day 2.
timeline_data_point: 8 US KIA total (7 enemy fire + 1 died of wounds)

event_description: Iran fires fresh missile barrage at Israel: Rocket trails seen over Netanya as Iran launches overnight ballistic missile salvo at Israeli territory. No direct impacts or injuries reported. Arrow and Iron Dome systems intercept all incoming. Fourth major Iranian salvo since Day 1.
timeline_data_point: All missiles intercepted

event_description: IDF strikes Hezbollah command centers in Beirut suburbs: Israeli airstrikes target Hezbollah command centers and weapons sites in Dahieh (southern Beirut suburbs). Four killed in strike on central Beirut apartment linked to Hezbollah operations. Lebanese displaced now exceeds 700,000.
timeline_data_point: 700,000+ displaced in Lebanon

event_description: NATO shoots down Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey: Turkey confirms NATO air defenses shot down a ballistic missile in Turkish airspace fired from Iran — the first such incident in NATO history. The missile was likely targeting Israeli assets or a wayward retaliatory launch. Turkey had previously deployed a Patriot system to protect a NATO radar station in eastern Turkey.
timeline_data_point: First NATO intercept of Iranian missile

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Haifa, Israel: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified) — Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
weapon_system: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified)
strike_notes: Hezbollah targeted missile defense site south of Haifa on March 2, first strikes since 2024 ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched drone swarm at Ramat David airbase targeting radar sites and control rooms. Direct Iranian missile fire at Haifa unverified — most Haifa-area attacks attributed to Hezbollah.

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 US KIA (Sgt. Benjamin Pennington, wounded March 1, died March 8). ~12 personnel suffered concussions and shrapnel wounds.
location_name: Prince Sultan Air Base, Al-Kharj, Saudi Arabia
strike_notes: 378th Air Expeditionary Wing. 2,300+ US troops stationed. Chinese satellite imagery showed 13 KC-135 tankers, 6 E-3 Sentries, 4 E-11 BACN aircraft pre-war. Saudi MoD confirmed intercepting multiple missiles. Iranian state media claimed hits on command centers and fuel storage (unconfirmed by CENTCOM). Sgt. Pennington was 7th US combat death of conflict. Pennington assigned to 1st Space Battalion, 1st Space Brigade.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

strike_notes: 90% of Iran's oil exports flow through Kharg. Struck in second-week escalation. Israel bombed 30 additional oil depots on Day 10. event_description: Tehran: Golestan Palace complex (UNESCO World Heritage Site — damaged by strike on Arg Square, Mar 2)
casualties_reported: Multiple incidents — see individual notes; 460+ killed in Tehran alone as of Day 11
strike_notes: Aggregated entry for confirmed civilian infrastructure damage across Tehran beyond the dedicated residential and military entries. Iranian MFA claimed 33 civilian locations struck nationwide including hospitals, schools, residential areas, the Grand Bazaar, Golestan Palace, and Azadi Stadium. Iranian Red Crescent reported 3,600+ civilian sites damaged as of Day 6; 20,000 civilian buildings including 16,000 residential units affected as of Day 11. WHO verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran (4 healthcare workers killed, 25 injured, 4 ambulances affected). 77 healthcare facilities affected per Red Crescent; 31 major clinical facilities/hospitals damaged and 12 rendered inactive per Deputy Health Minister. Niloofar Square strike (Day 3) killed 20 civilians per state media.

event_description: Shiraz (South): Underground ballistic missile production and storage facility south of Shiraz
strike_notes: Separate from the shiraz-airbase entry which covers Day 1-2 strikes. Satellite imagery March 7 showed ground-penetrating munitions used on underground facilities at a missile base south of Shiraz — one of at least 25 Iranian medium-range ballistic missile bases. June 2025 strikes had caused only light above-ground damage. IDF announced on March 13 that underground missile production site in Shiraz was struck. NCRI reports heavy strikes on Shiraz from early March 13, including airport, control tower, Gulf Highway, Moharrab Street, and SaIran complex.

event_description: Cyberattack disrupts Iranian oil export tracking systems: Iranian state media reports a cyberattack has disrupted the National Iranian Oil Company's logistics and tracking systems. Though Iran's oil export infrastructure is already physically destroyed, the attack complicates coordination of remaining domestic fuel distribution. Attribution unclear — likely US or Israeli.

NATO invokes Article 4 consultations over Turkey missile incident: NATO convenes emergency Article 4 consultations after Turkey's request following the intercept of an Iranian ballistic missile in Turkish airspace. While stopping short of Article 5 collective defense, allies agree to deploy additional Patriot batteries to southeastern Turkey and upgrade AWACS coverage. Iran warns Turkey of 'severe consequences' for 'siding with the aggressors.'
timeline_data_point: First Article 4 invocation over direct Iranian threat

event_description: Bipartisan group of senators demands AUMF vote: A bipartisan group of 34 senators — 18 Democrats and 16 Republicans — sends a letter to Senate leadership demanding a vote on Authorization for Use of Military Force within 72 hours. Senator Tim Kaine calls the war 'unconstitutional' without Congressional authorization. White House cites the 2001 AUMF and Article II powers.
timeline_data_point: 34 senators demand war authorization vote

event_description: China proposes 72-hour ceasefire at UNSC — US vetoes: China introduces a UN Security Council resolution calling for an immediate 72-hour humanitarian ceasefire and negotiations. Russia co-sponsors. France and UK abstain. US vetoes, stating it will not accept conditions that allow Iran to 'reconstitute its nuclear program or rearm Hezbollah.' Ambassador Linda Thomas-Greenfield calls the resolution 'a shield for Iranian aggression.'
timeline_data_point: US vetoes ceasefire resolution

event_description: Oil rebounds as markets reassess fundamentals: Brent crude surges back above $100 to close at $100.11/bbl (intraday range $92.89–$101.53) as traders recognize that yesterday's crash was driven by a false claim, not a genuine change in supply conditions. The Strait of Hormuz remains closed, 1,000+ ships remain stranded, and the 12.35 MBD shortfall persists. WTI closes at $95.28 (+9.2%). Gold breaks $5,126/oz on safe-haven demand.
timeline_data_point: Brent $100.11 — back above $100. WTI $95.28. Gold $5,127/oz

event_description: Gas prices climb to $3.72/gallon — California breaks $5.00: National average gas price reaches $3.72/gallon, continuing its relentless climb despite yesterday's oil price dip. California becomes the first state to break $5.00/gallon since the war began, hitting $5.02. AAA warns that the oil rebound today means no near-term pump relief. Trucking industry warns of freight surcharges that will ripple through consumer goods prices.
timeline_data_point: $3.72/gal — +24.8% since pre-war. CA breaks $5.00

event_description: Iranian state media claims hospital hit in Ahvaz: Iranian state television broadcasts footage of a damaged hospital in Ahvaz, claiming a US airstrike struck the facility, killing 12 patients and staff. Pentagon states the target was an IRGC logistics hub 400 meters away and that the hospital damage was caused by secondary explosions. Independent verification not yet possible.
timeline_data_point: 12 killed per Iranian claims — under investigation

event_description: Iranian civilian toll rises past 800 — refugee flows to Turkey and Iraq begin: Iranian authorities report over 800 civilian deaths. UNHCR reports early refugee flows — approximately 15,000 Iranians have crossed into Turkey's Van Province and an estimated 8,000 into Iraqi Kurdistan. Turkey warns it cannot absorb a mass displacement event and begins reinforcing its eastern border.
timeline_data_point: 800+ Iranian civilian dead. 23,000+ refugees

Day 13 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 20. Context: Day 13 — Zarzir strike (~60 wounded); 2 workers killed in Oman; IDF ground war in Lebanon
Day 13 estimated killed — US (military): 6. Context: Day 13 — Zarzir strike (~60 wounded); 2 workers killed in Oman; IDF ground war in Lebanon
Day 13 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 85. Context: Day 13 — Zarzir strike (~60 wounded); 2 workers killed in Oman; IDF ground war in Lebanon
Day 13 estimated killed — Israel (military): 4. Context: Day 13 — Zarzir strike (~60 wounded); 2 workers killed in Oman; IDF ground war in Lebanon
Western Iraq (crash site): N/A — aircraft loss not due to hostile or friendly fire — 6 US crew members killed
weapon_system: N/A — aircraft loss not due to hostile or friendly fire
strike_notes: US KC-135 refueling aircraft lost over western Iraq on March 12. All 6 crew confirmed dead by March 13. CENTCOM confirmed loss was NOT due to hostile fire or friendly fire. Under investigation. Brings total US military deaths in conflict to 13. KC-135s from Prince Sultan Air Base form backbone of strike operations — loss highlights vulnerability of tanker fleet.

event_description: IDF launches ground incursion into southern Lebanon: Israeli armored and infantry units cross the Blue Line into southern Lebanon in what the IDF describes as a 'limited ground operation' to push Hezbollah back from the border. Golani Brigade and 7th Armored Brigade spearhead the advance toward the Litani River. Heavy fighting reported near Marjayoun. Hezbollah's Radwan Force engages with ATGMs, destroying at least 2 Merkava tanks.
timeline_data_point: First Israeli ground operation in Lebanon since 2006

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Haifa, Israel: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified) — Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
weapon_system: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified)
strike_notes: Hezbollah targeted missile defense site south of Haifa on March 2, first strikes since 2024 ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched drone swarm at Ramat David airbase targeting radar sites and control rooms. Direct Iranian missile fire at Haifa unverified — most Haifa-area attacks attributed to Hezbollah.

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. event_description: Senate begins heated AUMF debate: The US Senate takes up the Authorization for Use of Military Force resolution demanded by 34 senators. Floor debate is heated — supporters argue the war is unconstitutional without explicit Congressional authorization; opponents call the debate 'aiding the enemy during wartime.' White House issues statement reaffirming Article II authority. Vote expected within 24 hours. Markets watch closely — an AUMF rejection could force a withdrawal timeline.
timeline_data_point: AUMF floor debate begins — vote expected March 14

event_description: Russia introduces rival ceasefire resolution at UNSC: Russia introduces its own ceasefire resolution at the UN Security Council, calling for an immediate cessation of hostilities, withdrawal of all foreign forces from Iranian territory, and an international conference on Persian Gulf security. Unlike China's vetoed resolution, Russia's draft includes language condemning 'unilateral military aggression.' US, UK, and France expected to veto. Diplomatic paralysis at the UNSC deepens.
timeline_data_point: Second ceasefire resolution in 48 hours — US veto expected

event_description: EU announces sanctions package on IRGC and Iranian oil: The European Union announces a comprehensive sanctions package targeting IRGC leadership, Iranian oil exports, and entities supporting Iran's ballistic missile program. The EU simultaneously calls for an 'immediate humanitarian pause' and offers to mediate. Iran responds by recalling its ambassadors from France, Germany, and the Netherlands. The sanctions signal Europe is aligning with the US position while maintaining diplomatic distance.
timeline_data_point: EU sanctions on IRGC + Iranian oil — Iran recalls 3 ambassadors

event_description: Oil eases slightly — Brent to $98.82 as convoy success tempers fears: Brent crude dips 1.3% to $98.82 as the second successful convoy transit and destruction of IRGC coastal batteries provide modest reassurance. WTI holds flat at $95.28. Analysts caution that the psychological impact of convoys exceeds the material impact — 5 tankers cannot replace 50. The fundamental shortfall of 12.35 MBD persists. Gold eases to $5,080/oz.

S&P 500 closes at 6,672.62 — slight decline on continued uncertainty: Markets drift lower as the AUMF debate introduces political uncertainty. S&P 500 closes at 6,672.62, down 0.3% on the day and 3.0% below pre-war levels. Defense stocks remain elevated at +16.5%. Oil majors ease 1% with crude. Airlines extend losses to -24.5% as fuel cost fears mount. VIX holds at 27.5 — elevated but below crisis peaks.

Gas prices hit $3.82/gallon — trucking surcharges take effect: National average gas price climbs to $3.82/gallon — up 28.2% from pre-war $2.98. California hits $5.15/gallon. The trucking industry's war surcharges announced yesterday take effect today, adding 8-12% to freight costs. Grocery chains warn of price increases on perishables within 3-5 days. The war's cost is no longer abstract — it is arriving at every checkout counter in America.
timeline_source: AAA / GasBuddy / American Trucking Associations / YCharts
timeline_data_point: $3.82/gal — +28.2% since pre-war. CA at $5.15

event_description: Displaced in Lebanon surges past 850,000: UNHCR reports displaced persons in Lebanon have surged past 850,000 as IDF ground operations enter their second day. Humanitarian corridors from southern Lebanon are overwhelmed. At least 65 Lebanese killed today, bringing the total to 773+, including 102 children. Medical facilities in Tyre and Sidon are operating beyond capacity. ICRC requests immediate humanitarian access for trapped civilians south of the Litani River.
timeline_source: UNHCR / OCHA / ICRC / Lebanese Health Ministry
timeline_data_point: 850,000+ displaced — 773+ killed including 102 children
