Day 14 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 20. Context: Day 14 — Kharg Island struck; Fujairah fire; AUMF debate; 15,000+ targets
Day 14 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 14 — Kharg Island struck; Fujairah fire; AUMF debate; 15,000+ targets
Day 14 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 65. Context: Day 14 — Kharg Island struck; Fujairah fire; AUMF debate; 15,000+ targets
Day 14 estimated killed — Israel (military): 6. Context: Day 14 — Kharg Island struck; Fujairah fire; AUMF debate; 15,000+ targets

US retaliatory strikes destroy IRGC coastal batteries: US strikes hit IRGC coastal missile batteries and fast-attack-craft pens at Bandar Abbas, Qeshm Island, and three additional sites along the Hormuz coastline. Pentagon calls it a 'proportional response to ensure freedom of navigation.' Iran's ability to threaten the strait corridor is degraded but not eliminated — mobile launchers remain operational.
timeline_data_point: 5 IRGC coastal sites destroyed

event_description: Houthis launch cruise missile at US destroyer in Red Sea: Houthis fire a land-attack cruise missile at USS Gravely (DDG-107) operating in the southern Red Sea. The missile is intercepted by SM-2. A second salvo of 3 one-way attack drones is shot down by CIWS. No US casualties. The Red Sea front now requires continuous carrier strike group presence, straining the Navy's two-theater deployment.
timeline_data_point: 1 cruise missile + 3 drones intercepted

event_description: IDF ground forces advance 5km into Lebanon — heavy fighting at Bint Jbeil: IDF ground forces push 5km north of the Blue Line into southern Lebanon on the second day of ground operations. Heavy fighting erupts near Bint Jbeil, a Hezbollah stronghold that was the scene of intense combat in 2006. Hezbollah's Radwan Force employs IEDs, anti-tank missiles, and tunnel positions. Six additional IDF soldiers killed — bringing the total to 10 since the ground incursion began. Hezbollah fires 300+ rockets at northern Israel in retaliation.
timeline_source: IDF / Al Jazeera / Hezbollah media office
timeline_data_point: 6 more IDF KIA — 10 total in ground operation

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Haifa, Israel: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified) — Casualties reported but Haifa-specific figures unconfirmed. Major Israeli casualties were in Beit Shemesh (9 killed) and Tel Aviv, not Haifa.
weapon_system: Hezbollah rockets and missiles / Iranian Kheibar Shekan MRBM (unverified)
strike_notes: Hezbollah targeted missile defense site south of Haifa on March 2, first strikes since 2024 ceasefire. Hezbollah also launched drone swarm at Ramat David airbase targeting radar sites and control rooms. Direct Iranian missile fire at Haifa unverified — most Haifa-area attacks attributed to Hezbollah.

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Tyre, Lebanon: Israeli airstrikes — Included in Lebanese total
strike_notes: Southern Lebanese city, Hezbollah rocket launch sites targeted. IDF issued evacuation orders for all residents south of Litani on ~Day 5, affecting 250-300 settlements. IDF 36th Division operating in southern Lebanon as of March 9. Limited incursions and active combat between IDF and Hezbollah confirmed in eastern sector near Khiyam and Ed Dhayra. Large-scale invasion south of Litani being planned but NOT YET LAUNCHED as of March 13.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: France and Italy open diplomatic talks with Iran: French and Italian foreign ministries announce preliminary diplomatic contacts with Iranian officials through Omani intermediaries. The talks represent the first direct Western diplomatic engagement since the war began. EU sanctions on IRGC and Iranian oil announced alongside the outreach. Iran had recalled ambassadors from France, Germany, and the Netherlands on March 13.
timeline_data_point: First Western diplomatic contact since war began

event_description: Gas reaches $3.675/gallon — AAA confirmed: AAA reports national average gasoline price at $3.675 per gallon, confirmed via OPIS credit card transaction data from ~130,000 stations. This represents a $0.695 increase (+23.3%) from the pre-war baseline of $2.98. The EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook (March 10) had forecast a March average of $3.58 — a figure already exceeded by mid-month.
timeline_data_point: $3.675/gal — +23.3% since pre-war $2.98

event_description: Iran Health Ministry: 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured: Iranian Health Ministry releases updated casualty figures: 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured since February 28. Separately, Hengaw (Kurdish human rights organization) reports 4,400+ Iranian military personnel killed, a significantly higher figure. Iranian Red Crescent reports 20,000+ civilian buildings affected including 16,000 residential units. 31 major hospitals damaged, 12 rendered inactive. WHO has verified 13 attacks on healthcare infrastructure in Iran.
timeline_source: Iranian Health Ministry / Hengaw / Iranian Red Crescent / WHO
timeline_data_point: 1,444 killed + 18,551 injured (Health Min.) — 4,400+ military (Hengaw)

Qatar intercepts 4 ballistic missiles — first evacuation orders in Doha: Qatar Defense Ministry intercepts 4 ballistic missiles and several drones targeting Doha. Large blast visible over Doha at approximately 1:30 AM from Patriot intercept. First evacuation orders issued in some Doha city districts. All incoming projectiles neutralized. Qatar has been targeted continuously since Feb 28 but this represents the heaviest single-night attack on the capital.
timeline_data_point: 4 ballistic missiles + several drones — all intercepted

event_description: 2,500 Marines ordered to Middle East — USS Tripoli deployment: The 31st Marine Expeditionary Unit (2,500 Marines) aboard USS Tripoli (amphibious assault ship) is ordered to the Middle East, pulled from waters near Taiwan. USS Nimitz deployment also extended. The Marine deployment signals preparation for potential Strait of Hormuz escort operations planned for late March. This represents a significant reallocation of Pacific-focused naval assets to the Persian Gulf theater.
timeline_data_point: 2,500 Marines (31st MEU) + USS Tripoli redeployed from Pacific

event_description: Fujairah oil hub fire — intercepted drone debris strikes bunkering facility: Debris from an intercepted Iranian drone strikes the major Fujairah oil bunkering hub in the UAE, starting a fire. One Jordanian citizen lightly injured. Oil-loading operations suspended at the facility. Fujairah is a critical alternative to Hormuz-dependent ports for oil exports via the Habshan-Fujairah pipeline.
timeline_data_point: Fujairah bunkering operations suspended

event_description: Ahmed al-Jaber Air Base struck — 3 Kuwaiti soldiers injured: Two Iranian drones strike Ahmed al-Jaber Air Base in Kuwait. Three Kuwaiti soldiers injured. Of 7 drones detected targeting the base, 3 were intercepted and 2 fell outside the threat zone. Kuwait has been targeted continuously since Day 1, with Ali al-Salem and Camp Buehring also receiving regular attacks.
timeline_data_point: 2 of 7 drones struck base — 3 soldiers injured

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: Fujairah Oil Hub, UAE: Drones — No casualties reported. Oil-loading operations suspended twice.
casualties_reported: No casualties reported. Oil-loading operations suspended twice.
strike_notes: Key strategic target — Fujairah is the terminus of the UAE's Habshan-Fujairah pipeline (1.5 mbd capacity), the primary bypass route for oil exports around the Strait of Hormuz. First attack March 14: intercepted drone debris struck bunkering facility, starting fire and suspending oil-loading. Second attack March 16: direct drone strike, oil-loading suspended again. Two shutdowns in three days signals deliberate Iranian strategy to close the Hormuz bypass. If Fujairah stays offline, the 12.35 mbd shortfall widens to 13.85 mbd.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: US strikes 90+ military targets on Kharg Island — oil infrastructure spared: CENTCOM executes large-scale precision strike on Kharg Island, targeting 90+ military objectives including naval mine storage, missile storage bunkers, air defense facilities, naval base, airport, and helicopter hangar. Oil infrastructure deliberately preserved. Trump warns he will 'reconsider' sparing oil assets if Iran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz. Trump called it 'one of the most powerful bombing raids in the History of the Middle East.' Iran FM vows retaliation.
timeline_source: CENTCOM / Trump Truth Social / NPR / CNN / Washington Post
timeline_data_point: 90+ military targets struck — oil infrastructure deliberately spared

Iranian FM vows retaliation for the March 13 Kharg strikes. The USS Nimitz deployment is extended. The rhetoric signals continued US pressure on Iran to reopen the Strait while the military prepares for escort operations in late March.

event_description: Estimated war cost reaches $20 billion — conflict enters third week: Extrapolating from the Pentagon's confirmed $11.3B through Day 6 and CSIS's $16.5B through Day 12 at a daily run rate of approximately $850M, cumulative direct military costs are estimated to have reached approximately $20 billion as of Day 16. Penn Wharton projects total direct budgetary costs of $40-95 billion (central estimate ~$65B) if the conflict lasts two months. The Pentagon is preparing a ~$50B supplemental funding request. Additional US economic losses from energy disruption estimated at $50-210B by Penn Wharton.
timeline_source: Pentagon / CSIS (Cancian & Park) / Penn Wharton / NYT / Military Times
timeline_data_point: ~$20B cumulative — $50B supplemental request pending

event_description: Gas estimated at ~$3.69/gallon — weekend data: Based on interpolation from AAA's confirmed $3.675 on March 14 and the ongoing upward trend, national average gasoline is estimated at approximately $3.69 per gallon. The increase of ~$0.71 from pre-war represents a 23.8% jump in just over two weeks. GasBuddy called the week of March 2-9 the 'largest weekly gas price jump of the century.'
timeline_data_point: ~$3.69/gal estimated — +$0.71 / +23.8% from pre-war

event_description: Lebanese casualties reach 826+ killed — IDF ground operations continue: Lebanese Health Ministry reports cumulative death toll from Israeli strikes and ground operations has reached 826+ killed and 2,009+ wounded since March 2. At least 102 children among the dead. 850,000+ displaced. IDF ground operations in southern Lebanon continue with heavy fighting around Bint Jbeil and the eastern sector. Hezbollah's Radwan Force continues to employ IEDs, tunnel systems, and anti-tank missiles against advancing IDF forces.
timeline_source: Lebanese Health Ministry / OCHA / ACLED / CIR
timeline_data_point: 826+ killed — 2,009+ wounded — 850,000+ displaced

7 ballistic missile salvos target Israel — cluster munitions in central Israel: Iran launches 7 ballistic missile salvos since midnight targeting central Israel and Eilat. In the Holon area, a cluster bomb warhead injures three people: two men in their 50s lightly injured, one man in his 60s moderately hurt by glass and submunitions. Apartment damage and vehicle fires reported. A separate salvo at Eilat causes no injuries. Iran continues averaging ~10 attack waves per day despite 80%+ degradation of offensive capability.
timeline_source: IDF / Magen David Adom / Israeli media
timeline_data_point: 7 salvos — 3 injured in Holon by cluster munitions

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Australia and Japan refuse to send warships to Hormuz: Despite President Trump's public call for allied nations to deploy warships to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, both Australia and Japan decline. Australia's Defense Minister says the commitment is 'not appropriate at this time.' Japan's PM rules out sending naval assets. No allied nation has made a firm commitment to join US escort operations, underscoring the diplomatic isolation of the US-led war effort.
timeline_data_point: Australia and Japan refuse Hormuz warship request

event_description: Iran FM: 'We never asked for a ceasefire' — diplomacy stalls: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi declares 'We never asked for a ceasefire' and vows Iran 'is ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.' The Trump administration separately rebuffs ceasefire efforts by Middle Eastern allies. Oman's mediation attempt fails to produce talks. The Pope calls for an immediate ceasefire. China condemns the attacks. A US Senate vote to curb Trump's war powers fails 47-53.
timeline_data_point: Both sides reject ceasefire — Senate AUMF vote fails 47-53

event_description: 56% of Americans oppose the war — NPR/PBS/Marist poll: A new NPR/PBS NewsHour/Marist poll finds 56% of Americans are against the war with Iran. The figure represents a significant public opinion shift given that the conflict is only 17 days old. Combined with the failed Senate AUMF vote (47-53) and the approaching $50B supplemental request, domestic political pressure on the war effort is building.
timeline_data_point: 56% opposed — first majority-oppose poll of the war

event_description: Brent surges to $106.18 — markets react to Fujairah/Dubai attacks: Brent crude surges 3% to $106.18 per barrel on Monday as markets react to the second Fujairah attack and Dubai airport strike. The rise demonstrates that Iran's ability to target alternative oil routes keeps upward pressure on prices even as its main military capability degrades. S&P 500 futures trade up ~0.5% pre-market near 6,664. Oil options markets price 30% probability of Brent above $120 within two weeks.
timeline_data_point: Brent $106.18 (+3%) — S&P futures +0.5%

Second Fujairah drone attack — oil loading suspended again: Iran launches a second drone attack on the Fujairah oil hub in the UAE, just two days after the first strike caused fires on March 14. Oil-loading operations are suspended for the second time in three days. Fujairah is the key bypass route for oil exports around the Strait of Hormuz, making these attacks strategically significant in maintaining the effectiveness of the Hormuz closure.
timeline_data_point: Fujairah oil loading suspended twice in 3 days

event_description: Drone strikes Dubai International Airport — fuel tank hit, flights suspended: An Iranian drone strikes a fuel tank near Dubai International Airport, the world's busiest for international passengers. All flights are suspended for several hours. Emirates diverts flights to Abu Dhabi and Al Maktoum Airport; one Dublin-bound flight turns around over Egypt. Emirates resumes limited service after 10am local time. The attack on a major civilian hub represents significant escalation.
timeline_data_point: Dubai airport flights suspended — fuel tank struck by drone

event_description: 1 killed in Abu Dhabi missile strike — Kuwait airport radar damaged: A missile strikes a vehicle in Abu Dhabi's Al Bahyah area, killing one person — the first confirmed fatality in Abu Dhabi from the conflict. Separately, Kuwait International Airport sustains radar equipment damage from an Iranian strike. Both attacks demonstrate continued Iranian targeting of Gulf civilian infrastructure despite 80%+ degradation of offensive capability.
timeline_data_point: 1 killed in Abu Dhabi; Kuwait airport radar damaged

event_description: IDF 91st Division expands ground operations — Khiam targeted: The IDF announces the 91st 'Galilee' Division is pushing deeper into southern Lebanon to expand the buffer zone against Hezbollah. Intense fighting around the strategic town of Khiam, which sits on high ground overlooking northern Israel. At least 3 airstrikes hit Khiam. Israel planning to seize entire area south of Litani River. Defense Minister Katz compares operation to Gaza ground ops. Lebanon indicates readiness for direct ceasefire talks without preconditions.
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Times of Israel / Axios / CNN
timeline_data_point: 91st Division at Khiam — planning full Litani buffer zone

event_description: Mojtaba Khamenei reportedly wounded — Hegseth: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth states that Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has been wounded, though details are not provided. If confirmed, this would be the second consecutive Iranian Supreme Leader to be directly affected by US-Israeli strikes — his father Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the opening hours of the war on February 28.
timeline_data_point: New Supreme Leader reportedly wounded

event_description: Kuwait International Airport: Drone / missile — Minor injuries to airport workers. Limited material damage to passenger terminal. 1 girl later died from shrapnel injuries (March 4). Radar equipment damaged March 16.
strike_notes: Civilian airport struck by drone on evening of Feb 28. Kuwaiti authorities confirmed minor injuries and damage to passenger building. On March 4, Kuwaiti Ministry of Health reported death of a girl from shrapnel-induced injuries — likely related to airport or broader Kuwait strikes. On March 16, radar equipment damaged by Iranian strike.

event_description: Fujairah Oil Hub, UAE: Drones — No casualties reported. Oil-loading operations suspended twice.
strike_notes: Key strategic target — Fujairah is the terminus of the UAE's Habshan-Fujairah pipeline (1.5 mbd capacity), the primary bypass route for oil exports around the Strait of Hormuz. First attack March 14: intercepted drone debris struck bunkering facility, starting fire and suspending oil-loading. Second attack March 16: direct drone strike, oil-loading suspended again. Two shutdowns in three days signals deliberate Iranian strategy to close the Hormuz bypass. If Fujairah stays offline, the 12.35 mbd shortfall widens to 13.85 mbd.

event_description: Dubai International Airport (DXB): Drone — No casualties reported. All flights suspended for several hours.
casualties_reported: No casualties reported. All flights suspended for several hours.
strike_notes: World's busiest airport for international passengers struck by Iranian drone hitting a fuel tank on March 16. All flights suspended for several hours. Emirates diverted flights to Abu Dhabi and Al Maktoum Airport; one Dublin-bound flight turned around over Egypt. Emirates resumed limited service after 10am local. Represents significant escalation — targeting the UAE's primary civilian aviation hub and a symbol of Gulf prosperity. Hundreds of thousands of travelers affected.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Tehran: Leadership House compound (Feb 28, Day 1)
strike_notes: Continuous bombardment since Day 1. Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in initial strike on Leadership House compound along with his daughter, son-in-law, grandchild, and daughter-in-law Zahra Haddad-Adel. Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh, IRGC commander Mohammad Pakpour, chief of staff Abdolrahim Mousavi, Ali Shamkhani, and multiple other senior officials also killed. Mojtaba Khamenei (son) injured but survived; named new Supreme Leader on Mar 8 (Day 9). Day 11 (Mar 10): 40+ killed in Resalat Square residential area strike in eastern Tehran (see tehran-east-residential). Day 12: IDF destroys IRGC Air Force HQ (see tehran-irgc-af-hq). 460+ killed and 4,300+ wounded in Tehran alone as of Day 11 per Tehran Emergency Health Department. Days 20-21: Ali Mohammad Naini (IRGC spokesperson) killed in IAF airstrike overnight Mar 19-20. Day 22: Continued strikes on Tehran military and government infrastructure. Day 24 (Mar 23): IDF launched 'broad wave of attacks' on Tehran. Day 25 (Mar 24): IAF struck 50+ targets overnight. Day 26 (Mar 25): IDF launches 'new wide-scale attacks' on Iranian government infrastructure, including Tehran targets. South Tehran strike kills at least 12, wounds 28. Eastern Tehran strikes destroy school and residential buildings.

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

event_description: NCTC Director Joe Kent resigns — says war started 'due to Israeli lobby pressure': National Counterterrorism Center Director Joe Kent resigned, stating the war was started 'due to pressure from Israel and its powerful American lobby.' Trump called it a 'good thing.'
timeline_data_point: First senior resignation citing Israeli influence

event_description: Trump renounces allied help — 'WE DO NOT NEED THE HELP OF ANYONE': Trump posted on Truth Social rebuking Japan, Australia, South Korea: 'WE DO NOT NEED THE HELP OF ANYONE.' Suggested leaving NATO. Every named ally has declined to send warships for Hormuz escort.
timeline_data_point: All allies declined — Trump threatens NATO departure

event_description: UK shares Hormuz coalition plan with allies: UK drafted a Hormuz escort coalition plan and shared it with the US and allies. However, UK has not committed its own forces. Plan shared via diplomatic channels.
timeline_data_point: UK plan drafted but no forces committed

event_description: Brent pulls back to $101 — S&P recovers to 6,735: Brent crude retreats 5% from Monday's $106.18 peak to $101.00, likely on profit-taking. Oil remains 42.3% above pre-war baseline of $71.00. S&P 500 recovers to 6,734.87, up 1.5% from its 2026 closing low of 6,632. Defense stocks ease slightly from highs. Oil majors pull back with crude. Airlines show modest recovery. Fundamentals unchanged: Hormuz closed, Fujairah intermittently attacked, 12.35 mbd shortfall persists.

War cost estimated at ~$21.7B — $50B supplemental not yet submitted: Cumulative estimated direct military cost reaches approximately $21.7 billion through Day 18, extrapolated from Pentagon's $11.3B through Day 6 and CSIS's $16.5B through Day 12 at ~$850M daily run rate. The $50B supplemental funding request led by Deputy Defense Secretary Steve Feinberg has not yet been formally submitted to Congress. Penn Wharton projects $40-95B total direct costs if conflict reaches two months, with additional $50-210B in economic losses.
timeline_data_point: ~$21.7B cumulative — ~$850M/day — $50B supplemental pending

event_description: Amnesty confirms US responsible for Minab school strike — 168-170 girls killed: Amnesty International published investigation confirming US responsibility for the Minab school strike on Day 1 (February 28). Revised death toll to 168-170 schoolgirls killed. Pentagon investigation still underway months later.
timeline_data_point: 168-170 schoolgirls confirmed killed — US confirmed responsible

event_description: Day 18 estimated killed — Iran (military): 60. Context: Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers; Ford fire contained; war cost ~$21.7B
Day 18 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 8. Context: Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers; Ford fire contained; war cost ~$21.7B
Day 18 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers; Ford fire contained; war cost ~$21.7B
Day 18 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 45. Context: Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers; Ford fire contained; war cost ~$21.7B
Day 18 estimated killed — Israel (military): 1. Context: Day 18 — Brent pulls back to $101; S&P recovers; Ford fire contained; war cost ~$21.7B

USS Ford fire contained — carrier fully operational after 30+ hour blaze: The non-combat fire aboard USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) that broke out March 12 in main laundry spaces has been contained after burning for more than 30 hours, per New York Times reporting. Two sailors sustained non-life-threatening injuries. Propulsion plant undamaged. Carrier remains fully operational and continues round-the-clock strike operations from the Central Red Sea. Ford has been at sea 265+ days, approaching an 11-month deployment record.
timeline_data_point: Ford fire contained — 265+ days at sea approaching deployment record

event_description: CRITICAL CORRECTION: Ford in Red Sea, not Eastern Med — naval disposition updated: USNI Fleet Tracker (March 16) and Defence Security Asia satellite analysis confirm USS Gerald R. Ford transited the Suez Canal on March 5 and is now operating in the Central Red Sea — NOT the Eastern Mediterranean as previously reported. USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) is deploying from the Atlantic to backfill Ford's vacated Med position. USS America (LHA-6) / 11th MEU has NO confirmed theater presence — actual amphibious asset is USS Tripoli (LHA-7) / 31st MEU, currently transiting South China Sea. Lincoln (CSG-3) repositioned to ~1,100km from Iran with 8 destroyers.
timeline_source: USNI Fleet Tracker 3/16 / Defence Security Asia 3/15 / USNI News
timeline_data_point: Ford in Red Sea — Bush deploying to Med — Tripoli replaces America

event_description: Three LCS mine countermeasure ships operating from Bahrain: Three Littoral Combat Ships — USS Canberra (LCS-30), USS Tulsa (LCS-16), and USS Santa Barbara (LCS-32) — confirmed operating Navy's first operational mine countermeasure mission packages in U.S. 5th Fleet out of Bahrain. Critical asset given confirmed Iranian mine-laying operations in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. has destroyed 30+ Iranian minelayers but Iran deployed 'a few dozen' mines from a stockpile of 2,000-6,000.
timeline_data_point: 3 LCS MCM ships — 30+ minelayers destroyed — dozens of mines laid

event_description: USS Tripoli ARG transits South China Sea — ETA Hormuz March 23-28: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) with 31st MEU (~2,500 Marines, BLT 3/1) continues transit through South China Sea en route to Middle East. Ship spotted sailing south of Taiwan through Luzon Strait on March 13. Naval News estimates 10-15 days to reach Strait of Hormuz area, placing ETA at March 23-28. The ARG includes CG-62 Robert Smalls, DDG-115 Rafael Peralta, and LPD-18 New Orleans. MEU brings F-35B strike capability and potential for island seizure operations near Hormuz.
timeline_source: USNI Fleet Tracker / Naval News / Stars and Stripes
timeline_data_point: ETA March 23-28 — 2,500 Marines + F-35Bs for Hormuz ops

event_description: Strait of Hormuz still closed — zero allied warships committed: Day 18 and the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed to Western-allied shipping. Maritime traffic down 97% from pre-war levels. ~1,000 ships anchored near strait entrance including ~200 oil tankers. ~3 shadow fleet/Chinese-flagged vessels transiting daily. Iran continues selective passage for non-Western vessels. No allied nation has committed warships to an escort coalition despite direct Trump pressure on Australia, Japan, UK, France, and Italy.
timeline_data_point: 97% traffic reduction — 0 allied warships committed — ~3 transits/day

event_description: Gulf infrastructure attacks — Fujairah (4th), Abu Dhabi, Umm Al Quwain, tanker struck: Iran continued attacking Gulf targets: Drone hit Fujairah Oil Industry Zone (4th attack since Mar 9). Drone struck Abu Dhabi Shah oil field. Missile killed Palestinian civilian in Al Bahyan, Abu Dhabi. Drone attacked Umm Al Quwain residential building. Kuwait-flagged LPG tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck 23nm east of Fujairah (17th vessel attack). UAE total: 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,672 drones since Feb 28. 11+ Gulf state civilian deaths (268+ injuries).
timeline_data_point: 17th vessel attack — 314 ballistic + 15 cruise + 1,672 drones at UAE

event_description: USS Tripoli transits Singapore Strait — ~1 week from theater: Reuters footage confirmed USS Tripoli (LHA-7) passing through Singapore Strait. Carrying 31st MEU (~2,200 Marines) with F-35B Lightning IIs (VMFA-121), MV-22B Ospreys, attack helicopters. Escorted by USS Robert Smalls (CG-62) and USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115). ETA operations area approximately one week. MEU capable of amphibious assault and maritime platform seizure.
timeline_data_point: Singapore Strait transit — 2,200 Marines — ETA ~1 week

event_description: Iran retaliatory barrage kills 2 Israeli civilians in Ramat Gan: IRGC launched '57th wave of Operation True Promise 4' — combined drone/missile attacks on Israel and Gulf states. Two Israeli civilians (couple in their 70s) killed in Ramat Gan by cluster-munition warheads from a ballistic missile. 25-year-old wounded by shrapnel in Bnei Brak. Tel Aviv train station damaged. Cumulative Israeli casualties: 16+ killed, 3,400+ wounded. ~50% of ballistic missiles now carry cluster warheads. 290 of 410-440 Iranian launch vehicles destroyed.
timeline_source: NPR / Times of Israel / PressTV / IDF
timeline_data_point: 2 Israeli civilians killed — 16+ total killed — 50% cluster warhead use

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

strike_notes: Fourth attack on Fujairah Oil Industry Zone since March 9. Drone strike sparked fires. Part of deliberate Iranian strategy to close the Hormuz bypass route via Fujairah. The Habshan-Fujairah (ADCOP) pipeline (1.5 mbd capacity) runs to Fujairah — repeated attacks have suspended oil loadings multiple times. Bunkering volumes down 60%+.

event_description: Shah Oil Field / Al Bahyan, Abu Dhabi: Drones / missiles — 1 Palestinian civilian killed in Al Bahyan by missile hitting civilian car. Fire at Shah oil field.
location_name: Shah Oil Field / Al Bahyan, Abu Dhabi
casualties_reported: 1 Palestinian civilian killed in Al Bahyan by missile hitting civilian car. Fire at Shah oil field.
strike_notes: Drone strike ignited fire at Shah oil field. Separately, a missile hit a civilian car in Al Bahyan district, killing a Palestinian national. Ruwais Industrial Complex (Abu Dhabi's largest refinery, 922,000 bpd) remains shut from earlier attacks. Cumulative UAE attacks: 314 ballistic missiles, 15 cruise missiles, 1,672 drones since Feb 28.

event_description: Umm Al Quwain, UAE: Drone — Fire in residential building — casualties unknown.
casualties_reported: Fire in residential building — casualties unknown.
strike_notes: Drone attack caused fire in a residential building in Umm Al Quwain. Part of broader March 17 Gulf attack wave.

event_description: East of Fujairah (maritime): Projectile — Minor structural damage, no casualties
strike_notes: Kuwait-flagged LPG tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck by projectile 23 nautical miles east of Fujairah at 23:01 UTC March 17. This was the 17th attack on commercial vessels since February 28.

event_description: Ramat Gan / Bnei Brak, Israel: Ballistic missile with cluster warheads — 2 elderly civilians killed (couple in their 70s, Ramat Gan). 1 wounded (25-year-old, shrapnel, Bnei Brak). Tel Aviv train station damaged.
actor_initiating: Iran (IRGC — '57th wave of Operation True Promise 4')
casualties_reported: 2 elderly civilians killed (couple in their 70s, Ramat Gan). 1 wounded (25-year-old, shrapnel, Bnei Brak). Tel Aviv train station damaged.
strike_notes: IRGC launched combined drone and ballistic missile attacks on central Israel in retaliation for Larijani killing. Cluster-munition warheads from ballistic missile killed couple in Ramat Gan. ~50% of ballistic missiles now carry cluster warheads to bypass Israeli defenses. Cumulative Israeli casualties: 16+ killed, 3,400+ wounded. Iran claims 'over 100 military and security targets' hit. 290 of 410-440 Iranian launch vehicles destroyed — daily missile volume down from ~100 on Day 1 to single digits by Day 18.

event_description: Australian airbase, UAE: Projectile — No Australian personnel injured per PM Albanese.
casualties_reported: No Australian personnel injured per PM Albanese.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: Israel kills Ali Larijani (SNSC Secretary) and Basij chief Soleimani: Israeli airstrikes killed Ali Larijani (Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council) and Gholamreza Soleimani (Basij paramilitary chief) — the third and fourth most senior Iranian officials killed since the war began. Confirmed by Iranian state media. Funeral procession for Larijani drew thousands on March 18.
timeline_source: Al Jazeera / Times of Israel / CNN / CBS News
timeline_data_point: 3rd and 4th most senior officials killed — systematic decapitation

event_description: US destroys Hormuz coastal missile sites with 5,000-lb penetrators: CENTCOM announced US forces employed multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions against hardened Iranian anti-ship cruise missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz coastline. CENTCOM stated missiles 'posed a risk to international shipping.' No formal press releases since March 14.
timeline_source: CENTCOM via social media / Jerusalem Post
timeline_data_point: 5,000-lb penetrator munitions on hardened Hormuz missile sites

event_description: Israel launches ground invasion of southern Lebanon: IDF launched ground invasion of southern Lebanon, concurrent with Iran campaign. This expands the multi-front war to include a full ground operation beyond the air campaign that began March 2.
timeline_data_point: Ground invasion concurrent with Iran air campaign

event_description: Bandar Abbas: IRIN naval base
strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. CENTCOM reported that within 48 hours of war start, all 11 Iranian warships in the Gulf of Oman had been denied presence. Three commercial ships struck by suspected Iranian projectiles in the strait on March 11. Day 23: Aerial campaign continues (Day 4) with A-10 Warthogs and Apache helicopters. Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz Coastline: Hardened Iranian anti-ship cruise missile sites along Hormuz coastline
strike_notes: CENTCOM announced via social media that US forces employed multiple 5,000-pound deep penetrator munitions against hardened Iranian anti-ship cruise missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz coastline. CENTCOM stated the missiles 'posed a risk to international shipping in the strait.' No formal CENTCOM press releases published March 17-18; last official release dates to March 14.

event_description: Unknown location (Iran): Ali Larijani — Secretary of Supreme National Security Council
casualties_reported: Ali Larijani killed — confirmed by Iranian state media
strike_notes: Third most senior Iranian official killed since the war began. Funeral procession drew thousands March 18.

event_description: Unknown location (Iran): Gholamreza Soleimani — Basij paramilitary organization chief
casualties_reported: Gholamreza Soleimani killed — confirmed by Iranian state media
strike_notes: Fourth most senior Iranian official killed since the war began. Killed same day as Larijani.

event_description: Iran FM Araghchi: 'We need new arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz': Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi told Al Jazeera 'We need to design new arrangements for the Strait of Hormuz,' signaling Iran views the strait as its most powerful remaining leverage and will not relinquish control cheaply.
timeline_data_point: Iran signals Hormuz as primary leverage — no intent to reopen

event_description: Mojtaba Khamenei status unknown — not seen since March 8: Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen publicly since his appointment on March 8. Leaked audio suggests minor leg injury. Hegseth claims 'wounded and likely disfigured.' Trump questioned whether he is alive. No new public statement issued. Command authority in Tehran unclear — IRGC may be operating independently.
timeline_data_point: Not seen in 10 days — health unknown — IRGC possibly autonomous

event_description: Brent crude spikes to $108.78 on South Pars news — European gas TTF +7.9%: Brent spiked to $108.78/barrel (+5.6%) on South Pars strike before easing slightly on Iraq-Kurdistan pipeline deal news. WTI traded around $94-96. European gas benchmark TTF jumped 7.9% on South Pars fears. Bitcoin fell below $73,000. Pre-war Brent was ~$67-71/barrel; intraday conflict high was $119.50 on March 10. US gas averages $3.79/gallon (+27%), CA above $5, diesel $5.04 (+34%).
timeline_data_point: Brent $108.78 spike — TTF +7.9% — gas $3.79/gal — diesel $5.04

event_description: IMO emergency session — 3,200 vessels, 20,000 seafarers stranded: IMO convened emergency session in London reporting ~3,200 vessels and ~20,000 seafarers stranded west of the Strait of Hormuz. CBS/60 Minutes: ~700 ships including 400 oil tankers holding 200 million barrels. Lloyd's List: 60 VLCCs (8% of global compliant fleet). Secretary-General Dominguez: 'unacceptable and unsustainable.' Proposed non-binding safe maritime corridor resolution. Insurance premiums at ~5% of vessel value (100x increase).
timeline_source: Euronews / CNBC / IMO / CBS / Lloyd's List
timeline_data_point: 3,200 vessels — 20,000 seafarers — 60 VLCCs — 200M barrels stranded

event_description: Iraq-Kurdistan pipeline restarts at ~250,000 bpd — marginal but symbolic: Iraq resumed oil exports from Kirkuk to Turkey's Ceyhan port at ~250,000 bpd, with potential to reach 450,000 bpd. KRG PM Barzani cited 'extraordinary circumstances.' This represents only 6-13% of Iraq's pre-war southern export volumes (3.4 million bpd from Basra, now halted). Brent briefly eased 1.38% on the news.
timeline_data_point: 250,000 bpd — potential 450,000 — 6-13% of pre-war Basra exports

event_description: War cost reaches ~$22.5B — S&P 500 at 6,677 — energy war premium: Cumulative war cost estimated at ~$22.5 billion through Day 19. S&P 500 at 6,677.00 (-2.9% from pre-war). Defense and oil sectors surge on energy war escalation while airlines decline further. IRGC Gulf energy threats introduce new risk tier for markets.
timeline_data_point: ~$22.5B cumulative — S&P 6,677 — energy war reshapes markets

event_description: Kirkuk-Ceyhan pipeline restarted at 250K bpd — first flow in decade: The Kirkuk-Ceyhan oil pipeline restarted at 250,000 bpd, with potential for 450,000 bpd. First flow in over a decade following Baghdad-KRG agreement. Hydrostatic testing ongoing on 100km section. Represents only 6-13% of Iraq's pre-war Basra exports.
timeline_data_point: Kirkuk-Ceyhan restarted — 250K bpd — first in decade

event_description: Tehran residential strike kills at least 2 — Bushehr nuclear plant hit: Iranian state media reported early Wednesday strikes on northern Tehran neighborhood; emergency crews recovered two bodies. Separately, a projectile struck the Bushehr nuclear power plant — no injuries per IAEA/Rosatom. ~480 Russian citizens remain on site.
timeline_data_point: 2+ killed Tehran — Bushehr nuclear plant struck — 480 Russians on site

event_description: Day 19 estimated killed — Iran (military): 55. Context: Day 19 — South Pars struck; Khatib killed; IRGC threatens Gulf energy; Brent $108; 3,200 vessels stranded
Day 19 estimated killed — Iran (civilian): 10. Context: Day 19 — South Pars struck; Khatib killed; IRGC threatens Gulf energy; Brent $108; 3,200 vessels stranded
Day 19 estimated killed — US (military): 0. Context: Day 19 — South Pars struck; Khatib killed; IRGC threatens Gulf energy; Brent $108; 3,200 vessels stranded
Day 19 estimated killed — Lebanon (all): 50. Context: Day 19 — South Pars struck; Khatib killed; IRGC threatens Gulf energy; Brent $108; 3,200 vessels stranded
Day 19 estimated killed — Israel (military): 1. Context: Day 19 — South Pars struck; Khatib killed; IRGC threatens Gulf energy; Brent $108; 3,200 vessels stranded

IRGC threatens Gulf energy targets — names SAMREF, Jubail, Al Hosn, Ras Laffan: Following South Pars strike, IRGC issued 'urgent warning' declaring specific Gulf energy facilities as 'direct and legitimate targets in the coming hours': Saudi Arabia's SAMREF Refinery and Jubail Petrochemical Complex, UAE's Al Hosn Gas Field, Qatar's Mesaieed and Ras Laffan facilities. Civilians and workers urged to 'immediately leave.'
timeline_data_point: Named 5 Gulf energy facilities as imminent targets

event_description: US Embassy Baghdad attacked by explosive drones: Explosive drones targeted the US Embassy compound in Baghdad, triggering sirens. Explosion heard near the diplomatic compound.
timeline_data_point: Baghdad embassy compound attacked

event_description: Ford heading to Souda Bay, Crete — MCM ships in Malaysia — MCM crisis: USS Ford transiting to Souda Bay, Crete for 1+ week pierside repairs. ~200 sailors treated for smoke inhalation, ~600 displaced. ~267 days deployed, approaching post-Vietnam record. CRITICAL MCM GAP: 2 of 3 LCS MCM ships (Tulsa, Santa Barbara) spotted in Penang, Malaysia. 4 Avenger-class minesweepers decommissioned Jan 2026. Washington Institute: 16 MCM vessels needed, Navy has 7. Iran retains 80%+ of minelayers.
timeline_source: USNI / Navy Times / Reuters / Washington Institute
timeline_data_point: Ford down for 1+ week — 2/3 MCM ships in Malaysia — 16 needed, 7 available

event_description: Al Dhafra Air Base, UAE: Ballistic missiles / cruise missiles / drones — 8 total killed (2 armed forces, 6 foreign nationals). 131+ injured across UAE.
strike_notes: Mar 22-24: UAE intercepted 8+ UAVs and 3+ ballistic missiles. Day 25 (Mar 24): Ballistic missile targeting Abu Dhabi intercepted; 1 Indian national suffered minor injuries from debris. Cumulative damage includes hits on Al Dhafra AB, Dubai International Airport, Palm Jumeirah, Burj Al Arab, Jebel Ali Port. 8 people killed (2 military, 6 foreign national civilians). Habshan gas facilities shut down.

event_description: NSA Bahrain / NAVCENT HQ: Cruise missiles / ballistic missiles / drones — 2 killed. Bapco Sitra refinery under force majeure since March 9.
strike_notes: US Fifth Fleet / NAVCENT HQ. Day 26 (Mar 25): Bahrain air raid sirens activated. Cumulative since war start: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at US-hosting bases in Bahrain.

event_description: Tel Aviv, Israel: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions — 18 Israeli civilians killed total since Feb 28. 4,002+ injured. Thai worker killed by cluster munitions at Adanim (Mar 19). 3 women killed in West Bank salon (Mar 19).
weapon_system: Fattah hypersonic / cruise missiles / cluster munitions
strike_notes: Day 26 (Mar 25): Missile hits Tel Aviv street — air defenses fail to intercept. Significant property damage, vehicles destroyed. Missile alerts across Israel: sirens in Dimona (near Negev Nuclear Research Center), Eilat, Netanya, central Israel. IRGC fires 'precision-guided' missiles and drones at Israel. Total Israeli killed since Feb 28: 17-18 (15 civilians + 2-3 soldiers). 4,800+ wounded (Al Jazeera tracker).

event_description: Beirut / Southern Suburbs: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry) — 1,001+ killed including 118+ children. 2,584+ wounded. 1M+ displaced (~1/5 of population).
weapon_system: Israeli airstrikes (in response to Hezbollah entry)
strike_notes: Hezbollah entered war March 2 after Khamenei assassination. IDF struck 2,000+ targets in Lebanon since March 2, eliminating 570+ operatives including 220 Radwan Force members. Hassan Ali Marwan (new Imam Hussein Division commander) eliminated Mar 19 — predecessor killed prior week. Ahmad Abdallah (Hamas military wing commander) died of wounds from Mar 11 strike in Beirut. Hezbollah: 668 cumulative attack waves on Israel (daily avg 37, trending up). 54 attack waves on Mar 19 — among highest single-day totals. IDF issued widespread evacuation orders in southern Lebanon Mar 20. 1M+ displaced — UNHCR warns families struggling to find shelter.

event_description: Strait of Hormuz (maritime): Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries — 20+ confirmed incidents. 4+ seafarer fatalities. 8 bulk carriers (40%), 6 tankers (30%), 4 container ships (20%), 2 service vessels (10%) attacked.
weapon_system: Anti-ship missiles / drones / naval mines / explosive unmanned surface vessels / coastal batteries
strike_notes: UKMTO: 20+ confirmed maritime security incidents since Feb 28. Two additional vessels struck Mar 20. Only 21 tankers have crossed the Strait since Feb 28 vs 100+/day pre-war. Half of March transits by shadow fleet (Lloyd's List). Iran selectively allowing passage: India (2 LPG tankers), Pakistan, Turkey, Saudi each transited with individual approval. War risk premiums: 0.125% to ~5% of vessel value (40x increase). $5M per transit for $100M tanker. VLCC rates hit all-time record $423,736/day. GPS/AIS disruption: 44 injected signal zones, 92 denial areas. IMO emergency session Mar 18-19: negotiating safe maritime corridor for seafarer evacuation.

event_description: US Embassy, Baghdad: Explosive drones — No casualties confirmed. Sirens triggered.
strike_notes: Explosive drones targeted the US Embassy compound in Baghdad, triggering sirens. Explosion heard near the diplomatic compound.

event_description: Ras Laffan Industrial City, Qatar: Ballistic missiles — Extensive damage. LNG Trains 4 and 6 damaged (12.8 MTPA = 17% of Qatar's LNG exports). Pearl GTL offline.
casualties_reported: Extensive damage. LNG Trains 4 and 6 damaged (12.8 MTPA = 17% of Qatar's LNG exports). Pearl GTL offline.
strike_notes: CRITICAL — World's largest LNG production facility handling ~20% of global LNG supply. IRGC struck in two waves (March 18-19) in retaliation for South Pars strike. QatarEnergy confirmed LNG Trains 4 and 6 damaged — 12.8 MTPA, representing 17% of Qatar's LNG exports. Repair timeline: 3-5 years. Force majeure declared on contracts to Belgium, Italy, South Korea, China. Estimated revenue loss: ~$20B/year (~9% of Qatar's GDP). Pearl GTL (Shell-operated) also damaged — offline minimum 1 year. Qatar expelled Iran's military and security attachés within 24 hours. Wood Mackenzie: 'fundamentally reshaping the global LNG outlook.' TTF gas surged ~30-33%.

event_description: Bahrain: Ballistic missiles / drones — 1 Emirati soldier KIA (Mar 24). No Bahraini fatalities confirmed.
strike_notes: Day 25 (Mar 24): IRGC attacked US 5th Fleet HQ in Bahrain with missiles and drones. 1 Emirati soldier KIA alongside Bahraini forces — first confirmed UAE combat death in direct Iranian attack. Cumulative war total: 153 missiles and 301 drones intercepted.

event_description: CRITICAL: South Pars gas field struck — first upstream energy infrastructure hit: Israeli Air Force, operating with explicit US coordination, struck Iran's South Pars natural gas field infrastructure and the Asaluyeh processing hub in Bushehr province. Phases 3, 4, 5, and 6 taken offline to control fires, halting two refineries with combined capacity of ~100 million cubic meters/day of gas — roughly one-fifth of the field's total capacity. First strike on Iran's upstream energy infrastructure since Operation Epic Fury began, crossing a line Trump had previously drawn. Qatar condemned the strike — South Pars is shared with Qatar's North Dome reserves.
timeline_source: Bloomberg / Axios / Jerusalem Post / Saudi Gazette
timeline_data_point: ~100M cm³/day gas offline — first upstream energy strike — Qatar condemns

event_description: Israel kills Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — 3rd official in 24 hours: Israeli strike killed Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib — the fifth most senior Iranian official killed since the war began and the third in just 24 hours (after Larijani and Soleimani on March 17). Confirmed by Iranian state media.
timeline_source: NPR / Al Jazeera / Times of Israel
timeline_data_point: 5th senior official killed — 3 in 24 hours — systematic decapitation

Day 24 (Mar 23): One person killed and one wounded in Bandar Abbas from attack targeting Persian Gulf Broadcasting Center radio transmitter.

strike_notes: Key Strait of Hormuz control point. Continuous strikes to suppress coastal batteries. Day 14: Continued strikes on remaining coastal defense positions as part of Strait of Hormuz suppression campaign. event_description: South Pars / Asaluyeh (Bushehr Province): South Pars natural gas field — Phases 3, 4, 5, 6
strike_notes: CRITICAL ESCALATION — First strike on Iran's upstream energy infrastructure since Operation Epic Fury began. Israeli Air Force with explicit US coordination struck South Pars Phases 3/4/5/6 and Asaluyeh hub early Wednesday March 18. Two refineries taken offline with combined capacity of ~100 million cubic meters/day of gas — roughly one-fifth of the field's total capacity. South Pars produces 730 million cm³ of gas/day, supplying 70% of Iran's domestic gas. Qatar immediately condemned the strike. Trump claimed US 'knew nothing' (sources contradicted). Netanyahu confirmed Mar 19 Israel would comply with Trump's demand to stop further energy strikes. IAEA DG Grossi told NPR enrichment capabilities will partially survive, contradicting Netanyahu. IRGC issued 'urgent warning' naming SAMREF, Jubail, Al Hosn, Mesaieed, Ras Laffan as retaliatory targets. Brent spiked to $119.11 intraday; TTF gas +30-33%.

event_description: Tehran (Northern neighborhood): Northern Tehran neighborhood — target unknown
strike_notes: Iranian state media reported early Wednesday strikes on northern Tehran neighborhood. Emergency crews recovered two bodies. Part of continued bombardment of Tehran that has killed 460+ in the capital since Day 1.

event_description: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant: Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant — projectile strike
strike_notes: Projectile struck Bushehr nuclear power plant on March 18. No injuries reported per IAEA and Rosatom. ~480 Russian citizens remain on site. FLAG: single-source on specifics. Separate from earlier Day 1-2 perimeter strikes tracked under bushehr-nuclear entry.

event_description: Unknown location (Iran): Esmail Khatib — Intelligence Minister
casualties_reported: Esmail Khatib killed — confirmed by Iranian state media
strike_notes: Fifth most senior Iranian official killed since the war began. Third killed in 24 hours (after Larijani and Soleimani on March 17). Systematic decapitation of Iranian leadership continues without producing capitulation.

event_description: Riyadh emergency summit — 12 Arab/Islamic FMs — missiles intercepted during meeting: Saudi Arabia hosted emergency consultative meeting of 12 Arab and Islamic foreign ministers (Azerbaijan, Bahrain, Egypt, Jordan, Kuwait, Lebanon, Pakistan, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Syria, Turkey, UAE). Iranian ballistic missiles intercepted over Riyadh during the meeting itself. Joint statement called on Iran to 'immediately halt its attacks.' Saudi FM: 'almost nothing can re-establish trust.'
timeline_source: Saudi Press Agency / Reuters / Al Jazeera
timeline_data_point: 12 FMs meet — missiles intercepted during summit — Saudi confrontation posture

event_description: Iran FM rejects ceasefire — 'We believe in ending the war on all fronts': Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi told Al Jazeera: 'We don't believe in a ceasefire. We believe in ending the war on all fronts.' He rejected any suggestion Iran was seeking negotiations. Combined with the killing of Larijani — Iran's most prominent pragmatist — this effectively closes the most visible diplomatic channel for de-escalation.
timeline_data_point: Iran explicitly rejects ceasefire — no diplomatic channel visible

event_description: Joe Kent resigns as NCTC Director — highest-ranking Trump official to quit over war: Joe Kent, Director of the National Counterterrorism Center, resigned March 17 — the highest-ranking Trump official to resign over the war. A retired Green Beret with 11 combat tours and Gold Star spouse, Kent posted: 'Iran posed no imminent threat to our nation.' He told Tucker Carlson there was 'no intelligence' of an imminent attack. Now under FBI investigation for allegedly leaking classified information.
timeline_source: NBC News / Tucker Carlson / Joe Kent statement
timeline_data_point: Highest Trump official to resign — under FBI investigation — 'no imminent threat'

event_description: Brent crude spikes to $119.11 — highest of the war — on Gulf energy attacks: Brent crude surged past $118/barrel intraday, reaching $119.11 — the highest since the war began — as Iran's retaliatory strikes on Gulf energy infrastructure hit markets. Session range $109.21-$119.11. WTI approached $100. Diesel trading 'north of $180/barrel.' European gas TTF surged 23-35% to near €67/MWh — highest since early 2023.
timeline_data_point: Brent $119.11 intraday — TTF +35% to €67/MWh — diesel $180+/bbl

event_description: Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.50-3.75% — inflation forecast raised: The Fed held rates at 3.50-3.75% with one dissent (Miran favoring cut). Inflation forecast revised up to 2.7% from 2.5%. Powell: 'Higher energy prices will push up overall inflation, but it is too soon to know the scope and duration.' Seven of 19 FOMC participants projected no rate cuts in 2026. Market rate cut expectations pushed to April 2027.
timeline_data_point: Rates held 3.50-3.75% — inflation raised to 2.7% — cuts pushed to April 2027

event_description: Markets close at 2026 lows — Dow -768 — S&P 6,618 — Nikkei -3.38%: Dow Jones lost 768 points (-1.63%) to 46,225 — new 2026 low below 200-day MA. S&P 500 fell 1.36% to 6,624.70 — lowest closing level of 2026. Nasdaq shed 1.46% to 22,152. VIX at 25.09 (+12%). Global: Nikkei -3.38%, Hang Seng -2.02%, Euro Stoxx 50 -1.71%. Energy stocks outperformed: Occidental +36% Q1; ExxonMobil and Chevron at all-time highs. Brent settled at $100.82.
timeline_data_point: S&P 6,618 new 2026 low — Dow -768 — Brent settled $100.82 — VIX 25.09

event_description: War cost reaches ~$23.4B — gas $3.86/gal — 4.2M+ displaced: Cumulative war cost estimated at ~$23.4 billion through Day 20. Daily cost ~$900M ('largest strike package yet'). National gas average $3.84-$3.88/gal (+29% since pre-war). Diesel near $5.07/gal. Eight states above $4, three above $5. EIA revised 2026 forecast from $2.91 to $3.34/gal. Total displacement: 4.2M+ (Iran 3.2M + Lebanon 1M+). Internet at 4%.
timeline_source: Pentagon / AAA / EIA / CNN / OCHA / NetBlocks
timeline_data_point: ~$23.4B cumulative — $900M/day — gas $3.86 — 4.2M displaced — internet 4%

event_description: Hengaw 5th report: 5,300 killed in 18 days — 511 civilians — 120 children: Hengaw Organization's 5th report documents 5,300 killed in the first 18 days of the war — 511 civilians (9.6%), including 120 minors and 160 women, plus 4,800+ military personnel. Strikes recorded in 178 cities across 25 provinces. Iran Health Ministry separately reports 1,444 killed and 18,551 injured. HRANA documented 3,114 deaths. US: 13 KIA, ~200 wounded.
timeline_source: Hengaw Organization / Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / DoD
timeline_data_point: Hengaw: 5,300 killed — 511 civilian — 120 children — 178 cities — 25 provinces
